definition: Based on illusion; not real or true; deceptive.
vietnamese: Ảo tưởng; không có thật; lừa dối
example: The oasis in the desert turned out to be illusory, disappearing as they approached.

definition: Grand and impressive in appearance.
vietnamese: Uy nghi; hoành tráng; gây ấn tượng mạnh
example: The imposing cathedral dominated the city skyline for miles around.

definition: Clever, original, and inventive.
vietnamese: Khéo léo; độc đáo; sáng tạo
example: She came up with an ingenious solution to the engineering problem.

definition: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
vietnamese: Tỉ mỉ; cẩn thận; chính xác từng chi tiết
example: The surgeon was meticulous in every step of the delicate procedure.

definition: In its original condition; unspoiled or clean.
vietnamese: Nguyên vẹn; tinh khiết; chưa bị ô nhiễm
example: The national park preserved the pristine wilderness from development.

definition: Causing emotion or touching the feelings.
vietnamese: Gây xúc động; chạm đến cảm xúc
example: The documentary told an affecting story about refugees seeking a new home.

definition: To cause or give rise to (a feeling, situation, or condition).
vietnamese: Gây ra; tạo nên (cảm xúc, tình huống)
example: Years of unfair treatment engendered deep resentment among the workers.

definition: To make narrower, tighter, or smaller.
vietnamese: Thu hẹp; siết chặt; làm nhỏ lại
example: Tight regulations constricted the company's ability to expand overseas.

definition: High respect; to regard with great respect and esteem.
vietnamese: Danh dự; tôn kính; trọng vọng
example: The community gathered to honor the teacher who had inspired generations of students.

definition: To reject or refuse to accept.
vietnamese: Bác bỏ; từ chối chấp nhận; phủ nhận
example: The scientist repudiated the claims made in the flawed research paper.

definition: Spreading widely throughout an area or group of people.
vietnamese: Lan rộng; phổ biến khắp nơi
example: The smell of fresh bread was pervasive throughout the entire bakery.

definition: Believable or convincing.
vietnamese: Đáng tin; thuyết phục
example: The witness provided a credible account of the events that took place.

definition: Confident and forceful in expressing opinions or desires.
vietnamese: Quyết đoán; tự tin; dứt khoát khi bày tỏ ý kiến
example: Being assertive in negotiations helped her secure a better contract.

definition: Very great or intense; showing deep insight.
vietnamese: Sâu sắc; thâm thúy; có tầm nhìn sâu xa
example: Reading that book had a profound effect on his understanding of human nature.

definition: To expect or look forward to something.
vietnamese: Mong đợi; dự đoán trước; kỳ vọng
example: Scientists anticipate that temperatures will rise significantly over the next decade.

definition: To remove or take away; to retreat or pull back.
vietnamese: Rút lui; lấy lại; thu hồi
example: The company decided to withdraw its product from the market after safety concerns arose.

definition: To guide or control the direction of something.
vietnamese: Điều hướng; dẫn dắt; kiểm soát hướng đi
example: The manager steered the team toward a more efficient workflow.

definition: Public disagreement or heated discussion within a topic or field.
vietnamese: Tranh cãi; gây tranh luận sôi nổi
example: The new policy sparked controversy among educators and parents alike.

definition: The state of being introduced to or affected by something.
vietnamese: Sự tiếp xúc; phơi bày; trải nghiệm với điều gì đó
example: Early exposure to music can enhance a child's cognitive development.

definition: Reliance on or need for something or someone.
vietnamese: Sự phụ thuộc; lệ thuộc vào điều gì/ai đó
example: The country's dependence on oil imports made its economy vulnerable.

definition: Progress or development in a particular area.
vietnamese: Sự tiến bộ; phát triển trong một lĩnh vực
example: Advancements in medical technology have saved millions of lives.

definition: To extend the duration of something.
vietnamese: Kéo dài; gia hạn thêm
example: The doctors did everything possible to prolong the patient's life.

definition: To act against or neutralize the effect of something.
vietnamese: Chống lại; vô hiệu hóa tác dụng của điều gì đó
example: Exercise can counteract the negative effects of a sedentary lifestyle.

definition: To cause something to start or happen.
vietnamese: Kích hoạt; gây ra; châm ngòi cho điều gì đó
example: Loud noises can trigger anxiety in some people with PTSD.

definition: To calm or relieve pain or distress.
vietnamese: Xoa dịu; làm dịu cơn đau hoặc căng thẳng
example: She sang softly to soothe the crying baby.

definition: Having no particular interest or sympathy; unconcerned.
vietnamese: Thờ ơ; không quan tâm; bàng quan
example: He was indifferent to the outcome of the vote since it did not affect him.

definition: Disgusted or shocked by something.
vietnamese: Kinh tởm; ghê tởm; bị sốc bởi điều gì đó
example: She was revolted by the unsanitary conditions in the facility.

definition: Higher in rank, status, or quality.
vietnamese: Vượt trội; cao cấp hơn; ưu việt hơn
example: This fabric is superior to the cheaper alternatives in terms of durability.

definition: The process or result of joining two or more things together.
vietnamese: Sự hợp nhất; kết hợp hai hay nhiều thứ lại
example: The restaurant's menu was a creative fusion of Asian and Mediterranean cuisines.

definition: The act of sending someone away or rejecting something.
vietnamese: Sự sa thải; bác bỏ; gạt đi
example: Her dismissal of his concerns made him feel unheard and frustrated.

definition: A detailed analysis and assessment.
vietnamese: Phê bình; phân tích và đánh giá chi tiết
example: The professor offered a thorough critique of the student's essay.

definition: The process of making something simpler or easier to understand.
vietnamese: Sự đơn giản hóa; làm cho dễ hiểu hơn
example: The teacher's simplification of the complex theory helped students grasp it quickly.

definition: To say something again or differently.
vietnamese: Phát biểu lại; diễn đạt lại theo cách khác
example: He restated his argument using simpler language for the audience.

definition: To discover or identify the presence of something.
vietnamese: Phát hiện; nhận ra; phát giác
example: Scientists used advanced equipment to detect traces of water on Mars.

definition: To officially register as a member or participant.
vietnamese: Ghi danh; đăng ký tham gia chính thức
example: She decided to enroll in a night class to improve her coding skills.

definition: The importance, worth, or usefulness of something.
vietnamese: Giá trị; tầm quan trọng; sự hữu ích
example: She placed great value on honesty in all her relationships.

definition: To pay no attention to; ignore.
vietnamese: Bỏ qua; phớt lờ; không chú ý đến
example: The driver disregarded the warning signs and continued down the flooded road.

definition: To show or exhibit.
vietnamese: Trưng bày; thể hiện; phô bày
example: The museum displayed rare artifacts from ancient civilizations.

definition: Not related or applicable to something.
vietnamese: Không liên quan; không áp dụng được vào vấn đề
example: His comment about the weather was completely irrelevant to the business discussion.

definition: Serving to forbid or prevent something by making it difficult or costly.
vietnamese: Mang tính cấm đoán; quá đắt hoặc khó để thực hiện
example: The prohibitive cost of housing in the city forced many families to move away.

definition: Characteristic of and unique to something.
vietnamese: Đặc trưng; độc đáo; mang tính riêng biệt
example: Her distinctive voice made her instantly recognizable on the radio.

definition: To limit or restrict.
vietnamese: Hạn chế; ràng buộc; giới hạn
example: Budget constraints forced the team to abandon several promising projects.

definition: To weaken or damage gradually or insidiously.
vietnamese: Phá hoại ngầm; làm suy yếu dần dần
example: Constant criticism can undermine a child's self-confidence.

definition: Strongly attracted or fascinated by something.
vietnamese: Bị thu hút mạnh; bị mê hoặc; bị cuốn hút
example: The audience was captivated by the magician's breathtaking performance.

definition: Able to be foreseen or expected.
vietnamese: Có thể đoán trước; theo quy luật dự đoán được
example: The movie's ending was so predictable that most viewers guessed it early on.

definition: Damaged or impaired by age or use; old-fashioned.
vietnamese: Cũ kỹ; lỗi thời; bị mài mòn theo thời gian
example: The timeworn proverb still holds truth in the modern world.

definition: To make something more noticeable or prominent.
vietnamese: Làm nổi bật; nhấn mạnh; tô đậm thêm
example: The lighting in the gallery was designed to accentuate the textures of each painting.

definition: To correct or put right.
vietnamese: Sửa chữa; khắc phục; điều chỉnh cho đúng
example: The company took steps to rectify the billing errors that had affected customers.

definition: Not well known; unclear or difficult to understand.
vietnamese: Tối nghĩa; ít được biết đến; khó hiểu
example: The professor specialized in obscure medieval manuscripts few had ever read.

definition: Vague, unclear, or ill-defined.
vietnamese: Mờ nhạt; mơ hồ; không rõ ràng
example: His plan for the future remained nebulous, lacking any concrete details.

definition: Never done or known before; without previous example.
vietnamese: Chưa từng có; chưa có tiền lệ
example: The pandemic caused unprecedented disruption to global supply chains.

definition: Existing in a real, tangible form; specific and definite.
vietnamese: Cụ thể; rõ ràng; có thể nhận biết được
example: The proposal needs concrete evidence before it can be approved.

definition: An impressive or unusual sight or display.
vietnamese: Cảnh tượng ấn tượng; màn trình diễn ngoạn mục
example: The fireworks display was a spectacular spectacle that drew thousands of onlookers.

definition: The process of making a copy or duplicate of something.
vietnamese: Sự tái tạo; bản sao; quá trình sinh sản hoặc tái hiện
example: The museum sold high-quality reproductions of famous paintings in its gift shop.

definition: The quality of trying to appear more important or clever than one really is.
vietnamese: Sự kệch cỡm; ra vẻ quan trọng hoặc thông minh hơn thực tế
example: His pretentiousness alienated colleagues who found him difficult to work with.

definition: Something that changes appearance to hide identity.
vietnamese: Cải trang; che giấu danh tính; ngụy trang
example: She wore a disguise to enter the event without being recognized.

definition: Lasting for a long time; durable.
vietnamese: Lâu bền; trường tồn; tồn tại lâu dài
example: Their enduring friendship survived decades of separation.

definition: Easily seen or noticed; attracting attention.
vietnamese: Dễ nhận thấy; nổi bật; thu hút sự chú ý
example: The bright red car was conspicuous in the parking lot full of gray vehicles.

definition: Worthy of reward or praise.
vietnamese: Xứng đáng được khen thưởng hoặc công nhận
example: She is a deserving candidate for the scholarship given her outstanding achievements.

definition: Not sure or definite; doubtful.
vietnamese: Không chắc chắn; mơ hồ; đáng ngờ
example: The outcome of the experiment remained uncertain until the final results came in.

definition: Ambiguous; open to more than one interpretation.
vietnamese: Nước đôi; mơ hồ; có thể hiểu theo nhiều nghĩa
example: His equivocal response left everyone unsure of his true intentions.

definition: Able to convince or influence someone.
vietnamese: Có sức thuyết phục; có khả năng gây ảnh hưởng
example: Her persuasive arguments convinced the board to approve the new budget.

definition: Limited to a particular group; not shared.
vietnamese: Độc quyền; dành riêng cho một nhóm; không chia sẻ
example: The exclusive club only admitted members who had been personally recommended.

definition: Faultless; beyond criticism.
vietnamese: Không thể chê trách; hoàn hảo; không có lỗi
example: Her irreproachable conduct throughout the investigation earned her widespread respect.

definition: Leaving no doubt; clear and unambiguous.
vietnamese: Rõ ràng dứt khoát; không để lại nghi ngờ
example: The judge delivered an unequivocal verdict that put the matter to rest.

definition: Impossible to overcome.
vietnamese: Không thể vượt qua; không thể khắc phục
example: The mountaineers faced what seemed like insuperable obstacles before reaching the summit.

definition: Inevitable; unable to be avoided.
vietnamese: Không thể tránh khỏi; tất yếu phải xảy ra
example: Death is the ineluctable fate that awaits all living beings.

definition: To come before in time or order.
vietnamese: Đi trước; xảy ra trước; đứng trước về thứ tự
example: A formal introduction should precede the main presentation.

definition: To officially choose or appoint someone or something for a specific purpose.
vietnamese: Chỉ định; bổ nhiệm chính thức; đặt tên
example: The committee designated her as the lead spokesperson for the campaign.

definition: To care for and encourage growth or development.
vietnamese: Nuôi dưỡng; chăm sóc và khuyến khích phát triển
example: Good teachers nurture curiosity and creativity in their students.

definition: To prevent or make impossible.
vietnamese: Ngăn ngừa; loại trừ khả năng xảy ra
example: His lack of experience precluded him from applying for the senior position.

definition: To evaluate or estimate the nature, quality, or value of something.
vietnamese: Đánh giá; ước tính; thẩm định
example: Teachers use various tests to assess students' understanding of the material.

definition: Not changing; constant.
vietnamese: Không thay đổi; đều đặn; bất biến
example: The monk maintained an unvarying routine of meditation and prayer each day.

definition: Based on facts, not influenced by personal feelings or opinions.
vietnamese: Khách quan; dựa trên sự thật; không bị cảm xúc chi phối
example: A good journalist must maintain an objective perspective when reporting the news.

definition: To give or feel in return; respond mutually.
vietnamese: Đáp lại; trả lại; có sự tương hỗ
example: She reciprocated his kindness by helping him move into his new apartment.

definition: To add notes or comments to a text or diagram.
vietnamese: Chú thích; ghi chú thêm vào văn bản hoặc sơ đồ
example: Students were asked to annotate the poem with their observations and interpretations.

definition: To make equal or uniform.
vietnamese: Cân bằng; làm cho ngang nhau; bình đẳng hóa
example: The new policy aimed to equalize educational opportunities across all districts.

definition: Successfully reached a goal or completed a task.
vietnamese: Đã đạt được; hoàn thành mục tiêu thành công
example: She achieved her dream of becoming a published author after years of hard work.

definition: Unconventional or slightly strange behavior or style.
vietnamese: Kỳ quặc; lập dị; hành động không theo lẽ thường
example: The eccentric inventor wore mismatched socks and spoke to his plants every morning.

definition: Lacking in quantity or quality; insufficient.
vietnamese: Ít ỏi; thiếu thốn; không đủ về số lượng hay chất lượng
example: The family survived on a meager income of just a few dollars a day.

definition: Based on or in accordance with what is generally done or believed.
vietnamese: Truyền thống; theo thông lệ; phù hợp với chuẩn mực chung
example: He rejected conventional career paths and chose to become an artist.

definition: Made or done without method or conscious decision.
vietnamese: Ngẫu nhiên; không có phương pháp; tùy tiện
example: The winners were chosen by a random draw from all the entries.

definition: Strong and durable; difficult to break or deal with.
vietnamese: Cứng rắn; bền bỉ; khó khăn; kiên cường
example: It was a tough decision, but she ultimately chose to resign from her position.

definition: Extremely ugly or unpleasant.
vietnamese: Xấu xí ghê tởm; kinh khủng; rất khó chịu
example: The monster in the story was described as hideous, with scales and glowing red eyes.

definition: Impossible to solve or fix.
vietnamese: Không thể giải quyết; không có cách khắc phục
example: The conflict between the two parties seemed irresolvable after years of failed negotiations.

definition: Containing opposing or conflicting statements or ideas.
vietnamese: Mâu thuẫn với chính mình; chứa đựng những ý tưởng trái ngược nhau
example: His argument was self-contradictory, claiming to support freedom while restricting choice.

definition: Of vital importance; necessary.
vietnamese: Bắt buộc; cực kỳ quan trọng; cần thiết
example: It is imperative that all employees complete the safety training before starting work.

definition: Certain to happen; impossible to avoid.
vietnamese: Không thể tránh khỏi; tất yếu xảy ra
example: Some degree of risk is unavoidable when launching a new business.

definition: To persuade someone to believe or do something.
vietnamese: Thuyết phục; làm cho ai đó tin hoặc làm điều gì đó
example: She worked hard to convince her parents to let her study abroad.

definition: To divert attention away from something.
vietnamese: Làm mất tập trung; đánh lạc hướng chú ý
example: Loud music in the library distracted students who were trying to study.

definition: To change or vary irregularly.
vietnamese: Dao động; thay đổi không đều; lên xuống thất thường
example: Stock prices fluctuate daily in response to economic news and investor sentiment.

definition: To give evidence or bear witness, especially in a legal context.
vietnamese: Làm chứng; khai báo; cung cấp bằng chứng (đặc biệt trong tòa án)
example: Three witnesses were called to testify about what they saw on the night of the incident.

definition: A serious disagreement or argument.
vietnamese: Xung đột; mâu thuẫn; tranh cãi nghiêm trọng
example: The conflict between the two departments was resolved through mediation.

definition: To make fun of or ridicule.
vietnamese: Chế giễu; nhạo báng; bắt chước để chê cười
example: It is unkind to mock someone for their accent or cultural background.

definition: Informed or notified about something.
vietnamese: Được thông báo; được biết về điều gì đó
example: The manager was apprised of the situation before the press conference began.

definition: Showing no pity or understanding toward someone or something.
vietnamese: Không đồng cảm; không có lòng trắc ẩn; thờ ơ với nỗi đau của người khác
example: The unsympathetic landlord refused to give any extension on the rent deadline.

definition: Mentioned or referred to in a particular source.
vietnamese: Được trích dẫn; được nhắc đến trong nguồn tài liệu
example: The study was widely cited in academic papers on environmental science.

definition: Calmed or pacified by something.
vietnamese: Được xoa dịu; được làm nguôi cơn giận
example: The angry customer was placated by a sincere apology and a full refund.

definition: Scattered or spread over a wide area.
vietnamese: Phân tán; tản ra; rải rác trên diện rộng
example: The crowd dispersed quickly after the event was cancelled due to rain.

definition: According to the usual practices or traditions.
vietnamese: Theo thông lệ; theo tập quán; theo truyền thống
example: It is customary to bow when greeting elders in many Asian cultures.

definition: Lacking a clear shape or form; shapeless.
vietnamese: Không có hình dạng rõ ràng; vô định hình
example: The amorphous blob of clay slowly took shape under the sculptor's hands.

definition: Not taking sides; unbiased or impartial.
vietnamese: Trung lập; không thiên vị; không đứng về phía nào
example: Switzerland maintained a neutral stance during both World Wars.

definition: To specify a condition or requirement clearly.
vietnamese: Quy định rõ ràng; đặt ra điều kiện hoặc yêu cầu cụ thể
example: The contract stipulates that both parties must give 30 days' notice before terminating.

definition: To replace something, often by force or strategy.
vietnamese: Thay thế; chiếm chỗ; soán ngôi
example: Streaming services have largely supplanted physical video rental stores.

definition: Relating to music; having a pleasant sound.
vietnamese: Liên quan đến âm nhạc; có âm thanh dễ chịu; có năng khiếu âm nhạc
example: She had a musical laugh that made everyone around her smile.

definition: Helpful or good; producing a positive effect.
vietnamese: Có lợi; có ích; tạo ra hiệu quả tích cực
example: Regular exercise is beneficial for both physical and mental health.

definition: Misunderstood or interpreted incorrectly.
vietnamese: Hiểu sai; diễn giải không đúng
example: Her remark was misconstrued as criticism when she had only meant to offer advice.

definition: Causing or speeding up a reaction or change.
vietnamese: Xúc tác; thúc đẩy; khởi động một phản ứng hoặc thay đổi
example: The protest became a catalyzing event that led to sweeping policy reforms.

definition: Evaluating or judging the value or quality of something.
vietnamese: Đánh giá; thẩm định giá trị hoặc chất lượng
example: The art dealer spent hours appraising the newly discovered painting.

definition: Using something or someone unfairly for benefit.
vietnamese: Khai thác bất công; lợi dụng người khác hoặc tài nguyên
example: The documentary exposed companies exploiting cheap labor in developing countries.

definition: Converting something into money or a source of income.
vietnamese: Kiếm tiền từ; chuyển hóa thành nguồn thu nhập
example: Many content creators are monetizing their social media presence through sponsorships.

definition: Done by choice or free will, without being forced.
vietnamese: Tự nguyện; theo ý muốn tự do; không bị ép buộc
example: She voluntarily gave up her seat on the plane to a family with young children.

definition: Gently and with care or affection.
vietnamese: Nhẹ nhàng; trìu mến; với sự quan tâm yêu thương
example: The nurse tenderly bandaged the child's wound to minimize any discomfort.

definition: To make something less severe, serious, or painful.
vietnamese: Giảm nhẹ; làm bớt nghiêm trọng hoặc đau đớn
example: Wearing sunscreen can help mitigate the risk of skin damage from UV rays.

definition: To regard with great respect or reverence.
vietnamese: Tôn kính; sùng bái; kính trọng sâu sắc
example: In many cultures, elders are venerated for their wisdom and life experience.

definition: To satisfy fully, especially hunger or desire.
vietnamese: Thỏa mãn hoàn toàn; no nê; đáp ứng đầy đủ ham muốn
example: A large bowl of soup was enough to satiate her hunger after the long hike.

definition: Following established or traditional beliefs or practices.
vietnamese: Chính thống; theo tín ngưỡng hoặc phong tục truyền thống
example: He followed orthodox medical treatments rather than experimenting with alternative therapies.

definition: Unnecessary repetition or duplication.
vietnamese: Sự dư thừa; lặp lại không cần thiết; trùng lặp
example: The editor removed all redundancy from the report to make it more concise.

definition: Occurring repeatedly or periodically.
vietnamese: Tái phát; xảy ra lặp đi lặp lại; định kỳ
example: She suffered from recurrent headaches that interfered with her daily routine.

definition: An expression of dissatisfaction concerning something.
vietnamese: Lời phàn nàn; sự bày tỏ không hài lòng về điều gì đó
example: The customer filed a formal complaint about the poor service at the restaurant.

definition: Not disturbed or troubled by something.
vietnamese: Không bị làm phiền; không bị ảnh hưởng; điềm nhiên
example: She remained unbothered by the negative comments and continued her work.

definition: Uncertain or slow to act due to doubt or fear.
vietnamese: Do dự; chần chừ; không chắc chắn vì nghi ngờ hoặc sợ hãi
example: He was hesitant to speak in public, fearing he would forget his lines.

definition: Not important or significant.
vietnamese: Không quan trọng; không đáng kể; tầm thường
example: The minor errors in the report were inconsequential and did not affect the conclusions.

definition: Of great importance or significance.
vietnamese: Trọng đại; có ý nghĩa lớn lao; đáng ghi nhớ
example: The signing of the peace treaty was a momentous occasion in the country's history.

definition: Kind and generous.
vietnamese: Nhân từ; tốt bụng; rộng lượng
example: The benevolent donor funded scholarships for hundreds of underprivileged students.

definition: Authentic, real, or sincere.
vietnamese: Thật sự; chính hãng; chân thành
example: His genuine concern for his students made him one of the most beloved teachers.

definition: A place where things are stored or collected.
vietnamese: Kho lưu trữ; nơi tập hợp và cất giữ thông tin hoặc vật phẩm
example: The library served as a repository of historical records dating back centuries.

definition: A great difference or inequality.
vietnamese: Sự chênh lệch lớn; bất bình đẳng; khác biệt đáng kể
example: There remains a significant disparity in wages between men and women in many industries.

definition: To clear someone of blame or suspicion.
vietnamese: Minh oan; chứng minh vô tội; bảo vệ khỏi bị đổ lỗi
example: New DNA evidence vindicated the man who had spent years in prison for a crime he did not commit.

definition: A formal request, often signed by many people.
vietnamese: Kiến nghị; đơn yêu cầu chính thức, thường có nhiều chữ ký
example: Over ten thousand residents signed a petition opposing the construction of the new highway.

definition: A brief summary or general overview.
vietnamese: Tóm tắt; bản tóm lược ngắn gọn
example: The editor asked for a one-page synopsis of the novel before reviewing the full manuscript.

definition: An assumption or hypothesis without proof.
vietnamese: Giả thuyết; suy đoán không có bằng chứng
example: His argument was based on supposition rather than concrete evidence.

definition: To record or enroll officially.
vietnamese: Đăng ký; ghi chép chính thức
example: All new students must register at the administration office before attending classes.

definition: Obstructing or slowing the progress of something.
vietnamese: Cản trở; gây trở ngại; làm chậm tiến trình
example: Heavy traffic was impeding emergency vehicles from reaching the scene of the accident.

definition: Showing independence or uniqueness in thought or behavior.
vietnamese: Theo chủ nghĩa cá nhân; thể hiện sự độc lập và khác biệt
example: Her individualistic style set her apart from the more conformist members of the group.

definition: Not likely to cause disagreement or argument.
vietnamese: Không gây tranh cãi; được chấp nhận rộng rãi
example: The proposal to plant more trees in the city was largely uncontroversial.

definition: Made up of parts that are all the same kind.
vietnamese: Đồng nhất; cấu thành từ các phần giống nhau
example: The neighborhood was once homogeneous, but immigration has made it more diverse.

definition: Made up of different kinds or types.
vietnamese: Không đồng nhất; gồm nhiều loại khác nhau; đa dạng
example: The committee was heterogeneous, drawing members from various professional backgrounds.

definition: Based on ideas or theory rather than practice.
vietnamese: Lý thuyết; dựa trên lý luận hơn là thực tiễn
example: The theoretical model worked well on paper but proved difficult to implement in reality.

definition: To break into small pieces or collapse gradually.
vietnamese: Vỡ vụn; sụp đổ dần dần; tan rã
example: Without proper maintenance, the old building began to crumble.

definition: To surprise or frighten suddenly.
vietnamese: Làm giật mình; làm sợ đột ngột
example: A loud clap of thunder startled everyone in the quiet room.

definition: To make someone well-acquainted or knowledgeable about something.
vietnamese: Làm quen; giới thiệu để hiểu biết; làm cho thông thạo
example: New employees should familiarize themselves with the company's policies on their first day.

definition: To fail to notice or consider something.
vietnamese: Bỏ sót; không chú ý đến; nhìn qua mà không để ý
example: In her rush to submit the report, she overlooked several important typos.

definition: Not overly proud or boastful; humble.
vietnamese: Khiêm tốn; không phô trương; giản dị
example: Despite his many achievements, he remained modest and rarely spoke about himself.

definition: A small amount or scarcity.
vietnamese: Sự thiếu hụt; khan hiếm; số lượng rất ít
example: There is a paucity of research on the long-term effects of this new medication.

definition: Understanding or sharing someone else's feelings, especially sadness.
vietnamese: Sự đồng cảm; chia sẻ cảm xúc của người khác, đặc biệt là nỗi buồn
example: She sent a card expressing her sympathy to the family after their loss.

definition: Very weak or slight; insubstantial.
vietnamese: Mong manh; yếu ớt; không đáng tin cậy
example: The connection between the two events was tenuous at best.

definition: Mutually reliant on each other.
vietnamese: Phụ thuộc lẫn nhau; có quan hệ tương hỗ
example: In a healthy ecosystem, all organisms are interdependent.

definition: Firm; not giving way under pressure.
vietnamese: Kiên định; không nhượng bộ; cứng rắn dưới áp lực
example: Her unyielding determination helped her overcome every obstacle in her path.

definition: Complete and including everything necessary.
vietnamese: Toàn diện; đầy đủ; bao quát mọi khía cạnh
example: The report provided a comprehensive analysis of the economic situation.

definition: A sign or piece of evidence that suggests something.
vietnamese: Dấu hiệu; bằng chứng cho thấy điều gì đó
example: The rising unemployment rate is an indication of broader economic problems.

definition: Not often; infrequently.
vietnamese: Hiếm khi; không thường xuyên
example: She rarely missed a class throughout her entire four years of university.

definition: To support or strengthen something.
vietnamese: Củng cố; hỗ trợ; gia cố thêm
example: Additional research was needed to buttress the claims made in the original study.

definition: Hostile or opposed to someone or something.
vietnamese: Thù địch; chống đối; có thái độ thù nghịch
example: His antagonistic attitude toward new ideas slowed the team's progress significantly.

definition: Copied or reproduced by someone else.
vietnamese: Được bắt chước; được sao chép; được mô phỏng theo
example: Her unique painting style has been widely imitated but never truly replicated.

definition: A feeling or belief that something is wrong or likely true without proof.
vietnamese: Sự nghi ngờ; cảm giác hoặc niềm tin rằng có điều gì đó không đúng
example: The detective's suspicion was confirmed when fingerprints matched the suspect.

definition: Something accepted as true without proof.
vietnamese: Giả định; điều được coi là đúng mà không cần bằng chứng
example: His business plan was built on the assumption that demand would continue to grow.

definition: A division or separation into parts.
vietnamese: Sự phân chia; vách ngăn; chia tách thành nhiều phần
example: A glass partition separated the open office space from the meeting rooms.

definition: To prove something wrong or false.
vietnamese: Bác bỏ; chứng minh điều gì đó là sai
example: The scientist refuted the popular theory with a series of carefully conducted experiments.

definition: To leave a place empty or to give up a position.
vietnamese: Rời bỏ; trả lại; làm trống chỗ hoặc vị trí
example: Guests were asked to vacate their hotel rooms by noon on the day of checkout.

definition: To form a mental image of something.
vietnamese: Hình dung; tưởng tượng; tạo hình ảnh trong tâm trí
example: Athletes often visualize themselves winning before competing in important events.

definition: In an awkward or uncoordinated way.
vietnamese: Vụng về; thiếu phối hợp; không khéo léo
example: He clumsily knocked over the glass of water while reaching for his phone.

definition: Consistently and dependably.
vietnamese: Một cách đáng tin cậy; nhất quán; ổn định
example: The machine reliably produced high-quality parts with very little variation.

definition: An improvement or increase in quality or value.
vietnamese: Sự cải thiện; nâng cao chất lượng hoặc giá trị
example: The software update included several enhancements to the user interface.

definition: Something made up or invented; a lie or falsehood.
vietnamese: Sự bịa đặt; lời nói dối; điều bịa ra
example: The accusations turned out to be a complete fabrication with no factual basis.

definition: A surprising or unknown fact made known.
vietnamese: Sự tiết lộ; điều bất ngờ được khám phá; khải huyền
example: The revelation that the CEO had been embezzling funds shocked the entire company.

definition: Not qualified or allowed to do or be something.
vietnamese: Không đủ tiêu chuẩn; không được phép tham gia
example: She was ineligible to vote in the election because she had not registered on time.

definition: Unwilling or hesitant to do something.
vietnamese: Miễn cưỡng; không sẵn lòng; do dự
example: He was reluctant to accept help, preferring to solve problems on his own.

definition: The state of being uncertain or unstable.
vietnamese: Sự bấp bênh; không ổn định; dễ gặp rủi ro
example: The precariousness of her financial situation kept her awake at night.

definition: The quality of being very accurate or precise.
vietnamese: Sự chính xác tuyệt đối; độ chính xác cao
example: The watchmaker worked with extraordinary exactitude to assemble the tiny components.

definition: The ability to recover quickly from difficulties.
vietnamese: Sức bật; khả năng phục hồi nhanh chóng sau khó khăn
example: Children who develop resilience early are better prepared for life's challenges.

definition: The quality of not attracting attention.
vietnamese: Sự không nổi bật; không thu hút sự chú ý
example: Her inconspicuousness made her the perfect candidate for the undercover assignment.

definition: A natural liking or attraction to something.
vietnamese: Sự đồng cảm tự nhiên; cảm tình; mối liên hệ gắn kết
example: She had a natural affinity for languages and learned three fluently by age twenty.

definition: Clearly shown or demonstrated in something.
vietnamese: Hiển nhiên; thể hiện rõ ràng; biểu lộ
example: His nervousness manifested itself in the form of constant fidgeting.

definition: To conclude based on evidence and reasoning.
vietnamese: Suy luận; rút ra kết luận từ bằng chứng và lý lẽ
example: From the clues at the scene, the detective inferred that the crime had been planned in advance.

definition: To confirm or support a statement or idea with evidence.
vietnamese: Xác nhận; chứng thực; củng cố bằng bằng chứng
example: A second witness came forward to corroborate the original testimony.

definition: To examine carefully and in detail.
vietnamese: Xem xét kỹ lưỡng; nghiên cứu chi tiết
example: The auditors scrutinized every transaction in the company's financial records.

definition: To estimate or infer something based on known information.
vietnamese: Ngoại suy; ước tính dựa trên thông tin đã biết
example: Scientists extrapolated future climate trends from decades of temperature data.

definition: To reduce something to the smallest possible amount or degree.
vietnamese: Giảm thiểu; giảm xuống mức nhỏ nhất có thể
example: Using protective gear can minimize the risk of injury in the workplace.

definition: To represent or stand for something else.
vietnamese: Tượng trưng cho; đại diện cho điều gì đó
example: The white dove symbolizes peace in many cultures around the world.

definition: To hide or keep something secret.
vietnamese: Che giấu; giữ bí mật; ẩn đi
example: She tried to conceal her disappointment behind a polite smile.

definition: Widespread respect and admiration based on achievements.
vietnamese: Uy tín; danh tiếng được kính trọng rộng rãi
example: Winning the Nobel Prize brought great prestige to the university.

definition: Able to be trusted or depended on.
vietnamese: Đáng tin cậy; có thể phụ thuộc vào
example: A reliable employee is one who consistently meets deadlines and delivers quality work.

definition: Able to be checked or proven true.
vietnamese: Có thể kiểm chứng; có thể xác minh là đúng
example: The researcher only included verifiable facts from credible sources.

definition: Extremely small; tiny.
vietnamese: Vô cùng nhỏ; cực kỳ nhỏ bé; không đáng kể
example: The difference in performance between the two models was infinitesimal.

definition: To imitate or strive to equal someone or something.
vietnamese: Noi gương; cố gắng ngang bằng hoặc vượt qua ai đó
example: Many aspiring writers try to emulate the style of their favorite authors.

definition: To use up completely or to tire out.
vietnamese: Làm cạn kiệt; sử dụng hết; làm mệt lả
example: She exhausted all her savings trying to keep the small business running.

definition: Done quickly and without careful thought.
vietnamese: Vội vã; làm nhanh mà không suy nghĩ kỹ
example: A decision made hastily is often one that is later regretted.

definition: To happen at the same time as something else.
vietnamese: Trùng hợp; xảy ra cùng thời điểm
example: The festival coincided with the anniversary of the town's founding.

definition: To conflict or disagree with someone or something.
vietnamese: Va chạm; xung đột; bất đồng với ai hoặc điều gì đó
example: Their strong personalities clashed during the brainstorming session.

definition: Honesty and straightforwardness.
vietnamese: Sự thẳng thắn; thành thật; không vòng vo
example: She appreciated his candor in telling her the truth rather than what she wanted to hear.

definition: Deep respect or admiration.
vietnamese: Sự tôn kính sâu sắc; lòng ngưỡng mộ
example: The students listened in reverence as the celebrated author read from her novel.

definition: Careful good judgment that avoids risks.
vietnamese: Sự thận trọng; sáng suốt; tránh rủi ro nhờ phán đoán tốt
example: Financial prudence requires saving money even when income seems sufficient.

definition: Thorough and complete.
vietnamese: Kỹ lưỡng; toàn diện; đầy đủ không bỏ sót
example: The investigator conducted an exhaustive search of the property for evidence.

definition: Concerned only with the obvious or surface details.
vietnamese: Bề ngoài; hời hợt; chỉ chú ý đến bề mặt
example: Her analysis was superficial, failing to examine the deeper causes of the problem.

definition: Doubtful or uncertain.
vietnamese: Đáng ngờ; không chắc chắn; đáng đặt câu hỏi
example: The authenticity of the painting was questionable given its unclear provenance.

definition: Creative and full of new ideas.
vietnamese: Giàu trí tưởng tượng; sáng tạo; đầy ý tưởng mới
example: Her imaginative approach to teaching made even the most complex subjects engaging.

definition: Having the ability or skill to do something.
vietnamese: Có năng lực; có khả năng làm điều gì đó
example: She proved herself capable of leading the team through a very difficult period.

definition: Large in size, amount, or importance.
vietnamese: Đáng kể; lớn về kích thước, số lượng hoặc tầm quan trọng
example: The project required a considerable amount of time and financial investment.

definition: Unfairly favoring one side or opinion.
vietnamese: Thiên vị; có định kiến; không công bằng khi ủng hộ một phía
example: A biased report cannot be used as objective evidence in scientific research.

definition: Something that comes before and signals the approach of another.
vietnamese: Chất tiền thân; dấu hiệu báo trước; tiền thân của điều gì đó
example: Widespread inflation is often a precursor to an economic recession.

definition: Stubbornly sticking to beliefs without considering others' opinions.
vietnamese: Giáo điều; cứng nhắc trong niềm tin; không chịu lắng nghe quan điểm khác
example: His dogmatic insistence on one approach prevented the team from finding better solutions.

definition: Wavering between different opinions or actions.
vietnamese: Do dự; lưỡng lự; dao động giữa các ý kiến hoặc hành động
example: Her vacillating stance on the issue made it hard for others to trust her leadership.

definition: Boringly ordinary or predictable.
vietnamese: Tẻ nhạt; nhàm chán; thiếu sáng tạo; quá quen thuộc
example: Critics dismissed the film as banal, offering nothing new to the genre.

definition: Not sincere or honest.
vietnamese: Không thành thật; giả tạo; thiếu chân thành
example: His apology seemed disingenuous, as he made the same mistake shortly after.

definition: More than necessary or reasonable.
vietnamese: Quá mức; vượt quá giới hạn hợp lý
example: Excessive use of social media can negatively affect mental health.

definition: Done or existing alone.
vietnamese: Cô đơn; đơn độc; một mình
example: He preferred solitary walks in the forest to social gatherings.

definition: To make changes in order to improve something.
vietnamese: Cải cách; cải tổ; thực hiện thay đổi để cải thiện
example: The government announced sweeping educational reforms to improve student outcomes.

definition: Having the necessary ability or knowledge to do something well.
vietnamese: Có năng lực; đủ khả năng làm tốt một việc
example: A competent manager can handle crises calmly and efficiently.

definition: Representing the most perfect or typical example of something.
vietnamese: Điển hình nhất; tiêu biểu nhất; thể hiện bản chất cốt lõi
example: New York City is often described as the quintessential modern metropolis.

definition: To nullify or make ineffective; to deny the existence or truth of something.
vietnamese: Phủ định; làm vô hiệu; phủ nhận sự tồn tại hoặc sự thật
example: One poor decision can negate years of careful financial planning.

definition: To express strong disapproval or criticism.
vietnamese: Lên án; phê phán mạnh mẽ; chỉ trích gay gắt
example: The senator was formally censured for his misconduct by the ethics committee.

definition: To force someone to do something against their will.
vietnamese: Ép buộc; cưỡng bức; bắt ai đó làm điều gì đó trái ý
example: Employees should never be coerced into working overtime without proper compensation.

definition: To criticize or reprimand severely.
vietnamese: Phê bình gay gắt; quở trách nặng nề
example: The press castigated the official for his irresponsible public statements.

definition: A person who gives help, especially financial aid.
vietnamese: Nhà hảo tâm; người bảo trợ; người giúp đỡ tài chính
example: An anonymous benefactor donated one million dollars to the children's hospital.

definition: A basic structure underlying a system or concept.
vietnamese: Khung; cơ cấu nền tảng của một hệ thống hoặc khái niệm
example: The new curriculum was built on a framework of critical thinking and problem solving.

definition: Strange or mysterious, especially in an unsettling way.
vietnamese: Kỳ lạ; bí ẩn; đáng lo ngại theo cách khó giải thích
example: She had an uncanny ability to predict what would happen before it occurred.

definition: Self-satisfied and unconcerned, often to a fault.
vietnamese: Tự mãn; chủ quan; quá hài lòng với bản thân đến mức lơ là
example: Teams that become complacent after early success often fail to maintain their lead.

definition: Unpredictable or irregular in behavior or movement.
vietnamese: Thất thường; không đều đặn; khó đoán trong hành vi
example: His erratic driving alarmed the other motorists on the highway.

definition: Fair and unbiased.
vietnamese: Công bằng; không thiên vị; vô tư
example: A judge must remain impartial throughout all proceedings regardless of personal views.

definition: Careful watchfulness for danger or problems.
vietnamese: Sự cảnh giác; chú ý quan sát đề phòng nguy hiểm
example: Constant vigilance is necessary to protect computer systems from cyber attacks.

definition: Careful consideration or discussion before making a decision.
vietnamese: Sự cân nhắc kỹ lưỡng; thảo luận trước khi quyết định
example: After hours of deliberation, the jury reached a unanimous verdict.

definition: Distrustful of others' motives; believing people act only out of selfishness.
vietnamese: Hoài nghi; không tin vào động cơ của người khác; cho rằng mọi người đều ích kỷ
example: Years of broken promises had made her cynical about political reform.

definition: Having the opposite effect of what is intended.
vietnamese: Phản tác dụng; gây ra kết quả ngược lại với mục đích
example: Punishing children too harshly can be counterproductive, encouraging rebellion rather than compliance.

definition: Implied though not directly expressed.
vietnamese: Ngầm hiểu; được ám chỉ mà không nói ra trực tiếp
example: There was an implicit agreement between the two colleagues not to discuss the matter publicly.

definition: Unable to be challenged or denied.
vietnamese: Không thể tranh cãi; hiển nhiên; chắc chắn đúng
example: The scientific evidence for climate change is now considered indisputable.

definition: Mysterious or difficult to understand.
vietnamese: Bí ẩn; khó hiểu; mang tính đố mị
example: The Mona Lisa's enigmatic smile has fascinated viewers for centuries.

definition: Deserving respect because of age, wisdom, or character.
vietnamese: Đáng kính; đáng tôn trọng vì tuổi tác, trí tuệ hoặc phẩm cách
example: The venerable professor had trained generations of scholars during her long career.

definition: Unable to be explained or understood.
vietnamese: Không thể giải thích; không thể hiểu được
example: For no inexplicable reason, the engine stopped working perfectly just before the race.

definition: Capturing and holding one's attention completely.
vietnamese: Cuốn hút mạnh mẽ; hấp dẫn đến mức không thể rời mắt
example: The enthralling performance kept the entire audience on the edge of their seats.

definition: Threatening or suggesting danger.
vietnamese: Đe dọa; gợi lên cảm giác nguy hiểm
example: Dark storm clouds gathered in a menacing formation on the horizon.

definition: Determined and unwavering.
vietnamese: Kiên quyết; kiên định; không dao động
example: Despite the criticism she faced, she remained resolute in her decision to pursue reform.

definition: To replace something older or less effective.
vietnamese: Thay thế; thế chỗ điều gì đó cũ hoặc kém hiệu quả hơn
example: Electric vehicles are expected to eventually supersede gasoline-powered cars.

definition: To improve or make better.
vietnamese: Cải thiện; làm tốt hơn; làm giảm bớt vấn đề
example: New housing policies were introduced to ameliorate the living conditions of the poor.

definition: A strong dislike or opposition to something.
vietnamese: Sự ghét; ác cảm mạnh mẽ đối với điều gì đó
example: She had a lifelong aversion to spiders that made hiking in forests challenging.

definition: Not producing the desired effect.
vietnamese: Không hiệu quả; không đạt được kết quả mong muốn
example: The ineffectual treatment failed to stop the spread of the disease.

definition: To appear more important or noticeable than something else.
vietnamese: Che khuất; làm lu mờ; trở nên nổi bật hơn điều khác
example: Her outstanding performance overshadowed all other contributions at the event.

definition: To provide evidence to support a claim.
vietnamese: Chứng minh; cung cấp bằng chứng xác nhận một tuyên bố
example: The lawyer worked hard to substantiate her client's alibi with credible witnesses.

definition: To involve or require as a necessary part.
vietnamese: Đòi hỏi; kéo theo; bao gồm như là một phần cần thiết
example: Taking on the leadership role entails a significant increase in responsibilities.

definition: To hinder or obstruct progress.
vietnamese: Cản trở; gây khó khăn; ngăn cản tiến trình
example: Poor communication between departments hampered the company's ability to meet deadlines.

definition: To emphasize or highlight something.
vietnamese: Nhấn mạnh; làm nổi bật điều gì đó
example: The data underscores the urgent need for investment in renewable energy.

definition: Unnecessary or excessive.
vietnamese: Thừa thãi; không cần thiết; quá mức
example: She removed the superfluous details from her essay to make it more focused.

definition: To scatter or disappear gradually.
vietnamese: Tan biến; phân tán; tiêu tan dần dần
example: The morning fog began to dissipate as the sun climbed higher in the sky.

definition: To put an end to or prevent something.
vietnamese: Dập tắt; ngăn chặn; kiềm chế
example: The government moved to suppress dissent by controlling the media.

definition: Not believable or likely.
vietnamese: Không đáng tin; khó có khả năng xảy ra; thiếu thuyết phục
example: The witness gave an implausible account that contradicted the physical evidence.

definition: Peculiar to an individual; distinctive.
vietnamese: Đặc thù của cá nhân; kỳ quặc theo cách riêng biệt
example: Each chef has an idiosyncratic way of preparing dishes that reflects their personality.

definition: To refer indirectly to something.
vietnamese: Ám chỉ; đề cập gián tiếp đến điều gì đó
example: The speech alluded to past struggles without naming any specific incident.

definition: Impossible to separate or escape from.
vietnamese: Không thể tách rời; không thể thoát khỏi
example: Language and culture are inextricable — one shapes and defines the other.

definition: To undermine or overthrow.
vietnamese: Lật đổ; phá hoại từ bên trong; làm suy yếu
example: The rebels sought to subvert the established government through propaganda campaigns.

definition: To copy or reproduce exactly.
vietnamese: Tái tạo; sao chép chính xác; tái hiện
example: Scientists replicated the experiment multiple times to confirm the results.

definition: To calm or satisfy, often by giving in to demands.
vietnamese: Xoa dịu; làm hài lòng bằng cách nhượng bộ
example: The manager tried to appease unhappy customers with discounts and apologies.

definition: To confuse or perplex someone.
vietnamese: Làm bối rối; gây nhầm lẫn; làm khó hiểu
example: The magician's trick confounded even the most attentive members of the audience.

definition: To continue firmly or obstinately despite difficulty.
vietnamese: Kiên trì; tiếp tục dù có khó khăn; bền vững
example: She persisted in her research despite receiving little funding and support.

definition: Skilled or proficient in doing something.
vietnamese: Thành thạo; có kỹ năng cao; giỏi giang
example: She is adept at navigating complex negotiations and finding mutually beneficial outcomes.

definition: To spread or distribute widely.
vietnamese: Phổ biến; truyền bá; lan rộng thông tin
example: Social media platforms make it easy to disseminate information to millions of people instantly.

definition: To find a way around an obstacle or rule.
vietnamese: Lách qua; né tránh; tìm cách vượt qua rào cản
example: Some companies try to circumvent environmental regulations by operating offshore.

definition: To make an unpleasant feeling less intense.
vietnamese: Xoa dịu; làm giảm bớt cảm giác khó chịu
example: He tried to assuage her fears by explaining the procedure step by step.

definition: To communicate or make known.
vietnamese: Truyền đạt; thể hiện; làm cho người khác hiểu
example: A good writer can convey complex emotions with just a few carefully chosen words.

definition: To increase in strength or volume.
vietnamese: Khuếch đại; tăng cường; mở rộng
example: The speaker's microphone amplified his voice so the entire crowd could hear clearly.

definition: To publicly condemn or criticize.
vietnamese: Lên án công khai; tố cáo; phê phán gay gắt trước công chúng
example: Several human rights groups denounced the new law as an attack on civil liberties.

definition: To make legally invalid or cancel out.
vietnamese: Làm vô hiệu; hủy bỏ hiệu lực pháp lý
example: The court ruling nullified the contract, freeing both parties from their obligations.

definition: To criticize unfairly or belittle.
vietnamese: Hạ thấp; bôi nhọ; phê bình một cách bất công
example: He was known to denigrate his competitors rather than promoting his own strengths.

definition: Known or understood by few; mysterious.
vietnamese: Bí truyền; khó hiểu; chỉ người trong nghề mới biết
example: The professor specialized in the arcane rules of medieval contract law.

definition: No longer existing or functioning.
vietnamese: Đã không còn tồn tại; không còn hoạt động
example: The defunct airline left thousands of passengers stranded when it shut down overnight.

definition: Present everywhere at the same time.
vietnamese: Có mặt khắp nơi; phổ biến rộng rãi
example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern society, appearing in every pocket and purse.

definition: To speak slightly of; to belittle.
vietnamese: Coi thường; nói xấu; hạ thấp giá trị của người khác
example: It is unprofessional to disparage former employers during a job interview.

definition: To surpass or exceed.
vietnamese: Vượt qua; vượt trội hơn
example: Demand for electric vehicles has begun to outstrip the available supply.

definition: Unable to be touched or physically measured; abstract.
vietnamese: Vô hình; không thể đo lường vật lý; trừu tượng
example: The company's greatest assets were intangible ones like its brand reputation and loyal customers.

definition: Respectful submission or yielding to the judgment of another.
vietnamese: Sự tôn trọng; phục tùng lịch sự ý kiến hoặc quyết định của người khác
example: In deference to the elder's experience, the team accepted her suggestion without debate.

definition: Impossible to overcome.
vietnamese: Không thể vượt qua; không có cách giải quyết
example: What seemed like insurmountable difficulties eventually gave way to breakthrough solutions.

definition: To make larger or increase.
vietnamese: Tăng thêm; mở rộng; bổ sung thêm
example: She augmented her income by taking on freelance writing projects on weekends.

definition: False or not genuine.
vietnamese: Giả mạo; không chính thống; không xác thực
example: The report was based on spurious data that had been manipulated to support a particular conclusion.

definition: Very careful and precise; morally honest.
vietnamese: Tỉ mỉ; cẩn trọng; liêm chính về mặt đạo đức
example: A scrupulous scientist records all results accurately, even those that disprove their hypothesis.

definition: Able to be touched or felt; obvious.
vietnamese: Rõ ràng; có thể cảm nhận được; sờ mó được hoặc dễ nhận thấy
example: The tension in the room was palpable as the results were about to be announced.

definition: Causing or likely to cause disagreement.
vietnamese: Gây tranh cãi; dễ gây bất đồng
example: Immigration policy remains one of the most contentious issues in modern politics.

definition: Requiring great effort; difficult.
vietnamese: Gian khổ; đòi hỏi nhiều nỗ lực; khó khăn vất vả
example: The arduous climb up the mountain tested every member of the expedition team.

definition: An opinion or conclusion formed without complete evidence.
vietnamese: Phỏng đoán; kết luận không có đủ bằng chứng
example: Without more data, any claim about the cause of the disease remains mere conjecture.

definition: Having mixed or contradictory feelings.
vietnamese: Lưỡng lự; có cảm xúc trái chiều hoặc mâu thuẫn
example: She felt ambivalent about moving abroad — excited by the opportunity but sad to leave her family.

definition: Effectiveness in producing a desired result.
vietnamese: Hiệu quả; năng lực đạt được kết quả mong muốn
example: Clinical trials are conducted to test the efficacy of new treatments before they are approved.

definition: Occurring irregularly or occasionally.
vietnamese: Không đều đặn; thỉnh thoảng mới xảy ra; rải rác
example: There were sporadic outbreaks of violence throughout the ceasefire period.

definition: To confirm or support the truth or value of something.
vietnamese: Xác nhận; xác thực; kiểm chứng
example: Independent tests were run to validate the claims made by the product's manufacturer.

definition: Lacking a plan or purpose; random.
vietnamese: Thiếu mục đích; tản mạn; không có kế hoạch
example: He made only desultory attempts to study, resulting in poor exam performance.

definition: Stubbornly resisting authority or control.
vietnamese: Ngoan cố; bướng bỉnh; kháng cự mệnh lệnh
example: The recalcitrant student refused to follow the classroom rules despite repeated warnings.

definition: Optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation.
vietnamese: Lạc quan; tích cực dù trong hoàn cảnh khó khăn
example: Despite the setbacks, she remained sanguine about the project's eventual success.

definition: Unreasonably high (price or demand).
vietnamese: Quá đắt; quá mức; vượt ngoài giới hạn hợp lý
example: The exorbitant rent in the city center forced many young professionals to move to the suburbs.

definition: The quality of behaving or speaking in a careful, prudent way.
vietnamese: Sự thận trọng; biết giữ bí mật; khéo léo trong lời nói và hành động
example: The doctor handled the sensitive information with the utmost discretion.

definition: The quality of being open to more than one interpretation.
vietnamese: Sự mơ hồ; tính nước đôi; có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách
example: The ambiguity in the contract's wording led to a lengthy legal dispute.

definition: To become less intense or severe.
vietnamese: Lắng xuống; dịu bớt; giảm cường độ
example: The pain gradually subsided after she took the prescribed medication.

definition: To come together from different directions.
vietnamese: Hội tụ; cùng tiến về một điểm từ nhiều hướng khác nhau
example: Thousands of fans converged on the stadium hours before the match began.

definition: Fair and impartial.
vietnamese: Công bằng; không thiên vị; hợp lý cho mọi người
example: The mediator proposed an equitable distribution of the company's assets among all shareholders.

definition: To think about deeply or carefully.
vietnamese: Suy ngẫm; chiêm nghiệm; suy xét sâu sắc
example: She sat quietly to contemplate her next career move after receiving two job offers.

definition: To free or remove from a difficult situation.
vietnamese: Giải thoát; thoát khỏi tình huống khó khăn
example: It took years of negotiation to extricate the company from its contractual obligations.

definition: Understanding of a situation after it has happened.
vietnamese: Nhìn lại; sự hiểu biết rõ ràng sau khi sự việc đã xảy ra
example: In hindsight, they realized that better preparation would have prevented the crisis.

definition: A sign or warning of something to come.
vietnamese: Điềm báo; dấu hiệu báo trước điều sắp xảy ra
example: The arrival of migratory birds is a harbinger of the changing seasons.

definition: A perfect example or model.
vietnamese: Hình mẫu; ví dụ điển hình; khuôn mẫu hoàn hảo
example: She was held up as an exemplar of professionalism and dedication within the organization.

definition: Official permission or approval; or a penalty for disobedience.
vietnamese: Sự chấp thuận chính thức; hoặc hình phạt vì vi phạm
example: The UN imposed economic sanctions on the country for violating international agreements.

definition: Cautious observation or consideration before acting.
vietnamese: Sự thận trọng; xem xét cẩn thận trước khi hành động
example: Given the risks involved, circumspection was essential before making any investment decisions.

definition: Gradual reduction or weakening.
vietnamese: Sự hao mòn dần; sự giảm sút theo thời gian
example: The company relied on natural attrition rather than layoffs to reduce its workforce.

definition: To gather or collect a large amount.
vietnamese: Tích lũy; thu gom; tập hợp số lượng lớn
example: Over the decades, she amassed a remarkable collection of rare first-edition books.

definition: To show clearly or demonstrate.
vietnamese: Thể hiện rõ ràng; biểu lộ; chứng tỏ
example: His actions evinced a deep commitment to the cause he had championed for years.

definition: To give a false impression or contradict.
vietnamese: Phủ nhận; che giấu thực tế; gây ấn tượng sai lệch
example: Her calm expression belied the anxiety she felt just before taking the stage.

definition: The quality of being delicate or precise; a fine distinction.
vietnamese: Sự tinh tế; sự khéo léo; sự phân biệt tinh vi
example: The subtlety of her humor was often missed by those unfamiliar with the cultural references.

definition: To attract or tempt.
vietnamese: Cám dỗ; thu hút; lôi cuốn
example: The store enticed customers with a huge sale on popular electronics.

definition: Able to be seen or noticed.
vietnamese: Có thể nhận thấy; có thể cảm nhận hoặc quan sát được
example: There was a perceptible shift in tone after the manager entered the room.

definition: Allowed or permitted.
vietnamese: Được cho phép; hợp lệ theo quy định
example: The use of calculators is permissible during this portion of the exam.

definition: To prevent or delay something by taking action ahead of time.
vietnamese: Ngăn chặn trước; phòng ngừa bằng cách hành động sớm
example: She sent a preemptive email to forestall any misunderstandings about her intentions.

definition: Genuine or real.
vietnamese: Chính hãng; xác thực; có nguồn gốc thật sự
example: The museum confirmed that the painting was authentic after careful analysis.

definition: To be a perfect example of something.
vietnamese: Là hình mẫu tiêu biểu; đại diện hoàn hảo cho điều gì đó
example: Her tireless work ethic epitomizes the dedication required to succeed in medicine.

definition: To draw out a response or reaction.
vietnamese: Gợi ra; kéo ra; khơi dậy phản ứng hoặc câu trả lời
example: The teacher used open-ended questions to elicit thoughtful responses from her students.

definition: A distinguishing characteristic or feature.
vietnamese: Đặc điểm nhận dạng; dấu hiệu đặc trưng
example: Attention to detail is the hallmark of a truly skilled craftsperson.

definition: Done without enthusiasm or thoroughness.
vietnamese: Qua loa; hời hợt; làm cho có lệ
example: He gave a perfunctory nod to the idea without actually committing to it.

definition: Capable of working successfully.
vietnamese: Khả thi; có thể thực hiện được và thành công
example: The committee evaluated which proposals were financially viable before proceeding.

definition: To give up or reject formally.
vietnamese: Từ bỏ; tuyên bố từ chối chính thức
example: He renounced his citizenship to avoid paying taxes in his home country.

definition: Able to be exchanged or replaced.
vietnamese: Có thể hoán đổi; thay thế lẫn nhau
example: The two terms are often used as if interchangeable, but they have distinct meanings.

definition: Serving as a symbol.
vietnamese: Mang tính biểu tượng; đại diện cho điều gì đó
example: The bald eagle is emblematic of strength and freedom in American culture.

definition: Unclear or having multiple meanings.
vietnamese: Mơ hồ; không rõ ràng; có nhiều nghĩa
example: The politician gave an ambiguous answer that satisfied neither side of the debate.

definition: To hold back or restrain.
vietnamese: Ức chế; kiềm chế; ngăn cản
example: Fear of failure can inhibit creativity and the willingness to take risks.

definition: To escape or avoid.
vietnamese: Tránh né; lẩn trốn; né tránh
example: The thief managed to evade capture for several weeks before being caught.

definition: To list one by one.
vietnamese: Liệt kê từng cái một; đếm và kể ra
example: The report enumerated the specific steps needed to reduce carbon emissions.

definition: To shorten a text without losing the sense.
vietnamese: Rút gọn; tóm tắt văn bản mà không mất ý chính
example: The publisher decided to abridge the lengthy novel for a younger audience.

definition: To go beyond or exceed.
vietnamese: Vượt qua; vượt trội; hơn hẳn
example: The athlete surpassed the world record by nearly two seconds.

definition: To summarize or enclose in a capsule.
vietnamese: Tóm gọn; đóng gói; tổng kết trong một không gian nhỏ
example: The opening sentence of the essay encapsulates the author's main argument beautifully.

definition: To reduce or cut short.
vietnamese: Cắt giảm; rút ngắn; hạn chế
example: The budget crisis forced the government to curtail spending on public services.

definition: Courage in pain or adversity.
vietnamese: Lòng can đảm; kiên cường trước khó khăn và đau đớn
example: She showed remarkable fortitude in continuing her research despite numerous personal setbacks.

definition: Casual lack of concern; indifference.
vietnamese: Thái độ bình thản; sự thờ ơ nhẹ nhàng; vô lo
example: He accepted the award with an air of nonchalance that surprised many in the audience.

definition: A scarcity or lack of something.
vietnamese: Sự thiếu hụt; khan hiếm; sự thiếu thốn
example: There is a dearth of affordable housing in many major cities worldwide.

definition: Lack of harmony or agreement; conflict.
vietnamese: Sự bất hòa; thiếu thống nhất; xung đột
example: The cognitive dissonance of preaching honesty while lying created obvious stress for him.

definition: To make or become smaller or less.
vietnamese: Giảm bớt; thu nhỏ; làm yếu đi
example: His influence in the organization gradually diminished as newer leaders emerged.

definition: Deviating from what is standard or expected; abnormal.
vietnamese: Bất thường; lệch khỏi chuẩn mực; dị thường
example: The anomalous reading in the data prompted scientists to repeat the experiment.

definition: To twist out of shape or misrepresent.
vietnamese: Bóp méo; làm biến dạng; xuyên tạc
example: The media was accused of distorting the facts to make the story more sensational.

definition: To cause or bring about.
vietnamese: Gây ra; tạo ra; khuyến khích điều gì đó xảy ra
example: The doctor prescribed medication to induce sleep in the insomniac patient.

definition: To separate or disengage.
vietnamese: Tách rời; ngắt kết nối; giải phóng khỏi sự phụ thuộc
example: Economists debated whether it was possible to decouple economic growth from carbon emissions.

definition: To intervene to resolve a dispute.
vietnamese: Hòa giải; làm trung gian để giải quyết tranh chấp
example: A neutral third party was brought in to mediate between the two opposing factions.

definition: To hinder or obstruct.
vietnamese: Cản trở; gây trở ngại; làm khó khăn tiến trình
example: Bureaucratic red tape often impedes innovation and slows down business development.

definition: The ability to return to original shape after stretching; flexibility.
vietnamese: Tính đàn hồi; khả năng trở về hình dạng ban đầu; sự linh hoạt
example: The elasticity of demand determines how consumers react to price changes.

definition: Mutual exchange or cooperation.
vietnamese: Sự đáp lại qua lại; trao đổi tương hỗ; hợp tác hai chiều
example: Trade agreements are based on the principle of reciprocity between partnering countries.

definition: The act of sticking together or unity.
vietnamese: Sự gắn kết; tính thống nhất; liên kết chặt chẽ
example: Team cohesion is essential for achieving consistent performance in competitive sports.

definition: Strictness, severity, or thoroughness.
vietnamese: Sự nghiêm ngặt; tính chặt chẽ; độ kỹ lưỡng cao
example: Academic rigor ensures that research findings are reliable and reproducible.

definition: The quality of being new or unusual.
vietnamese: Tính mới lạ; sự độc đáo; điều chưa từng thấy
example: The novelty of the concept attracted a lot of media attention in its early days.

definition: Characterized by constant change or activity.
vietnamese: Năng động; không ngừng thay đổi và phát triển; sinh động
example: The tech industry is one of the most dynamic sectors of the global economy.

definition: Unchangeable over time.
vietnamese: Bất biến; không thể thay đổi theo thời gian
example: Some philosophers argue that moral principles are immutable, regardless of cultural context.

definition: Able to be maintained over time without depletion.
vietnamese: Bền vững; có thể duy trì lâu dài mà không cạn kiệt tài nguyên
example: The company committed to using only sustainable materials in its manufacturing process.

definition: Having clear, original ideas about the future.
vietnamese: Có tầm nhìn xa; người tiên phong với những ý tưởng mới về tương lai
example: The visionary entrepreneur imagined a world where clean energy replaced fossil fuels.

definition: A firm decision or solution to a problem.
vietnamese: Quyết tâm; nghị quyết; giải pháp cho vấn đề
example: The new year inspired her to make a resolution to exercise at least three times a week.

definition: Causing strong reaction or stimulating thought.
vietnamese: Khiêu khích; gây phản ứng mạnh; kích thích suy nghĩ
example: The documentary raised provocative questions about the ethics of artificial intelligence.

definition: Impossible to deny or disprove.
vietnamese: Không thể bác bỏ; chắc chắn đúng; không thể phủ nhận
example: She presented irrefutable evidence that cleared her client of all wrongdoing.

definition: To combine or unite into one.
vietnamese: Hợp nhất; kết hợp thành một; sáp nhập
example: The two rival newspapers amalgamated to form a single publication.

definition: To officially announce or make known.
vietnamese: Ban hành; công bố chính thức; tuyên truyền rộng rãi
example: The government promulgated new regulations governing the use of personal data.

definition: To combine into a more effective or coherent whole.
vietnamese: Củng cố; hợp nhất; kết hợp thành khối vững chắc hơn
example: The merger helped the company consolidate its position in the global market.

definition: Possible to do easily or conveniently.
vietnamese: Khả thi; có thể thực hiện được một cách thuận tiện
example: Engineers assessed whether a tunnel under the river would be technically feasible.

definition: The ability to make decisions quickly and firmly.
vietnamese: Tính quyết đoán; khả năng ra quyết định nhanh chóng và dứt khoát
example: In times of crisis, decisiveness is more valuable than prolonged deliberation.

definition: To publicly denounce or criticize.
vietnamese: Phê phán công khai; lên tiếng chỉ trích
example: Critics decried the policy as harmful to the long-term health of the economy.

definition: Resistance to change in motion or condition.
vietnamese: Quán tính; sức ì; kháng cự sự thay đổi
example: Organizational inertia often prevents companies from adapting to rapid market changes.

definition: The quality of being convenient and practical despite possibly being improper.
vietnamese: Tính tiện lợi thực tế dù có thể không hoàn toàn đúng đắn
example: He chose expediency over principle when he accepted the compromise agreement.

definition: Fertility or ability to produce abundantly.
vietnamese: Khả năng sinh sản dồi dào; năng suất cao; sự phong phú
example: The fecundity of the soil in the valley made it ideal for growing a variety of crops.

definition: To speed up or facilitate.
vietnamese: Đẩy nhanh; xúc tiến; tạo điều kiện nhanh hơn
example: She called the manager to expedite the processing of her visa application.

definition: Showing concern for others' feelings.
vietnamese: Chu đáo; quan tâm đến cảm xúc của người khác; tế nhị
example: Being considerate of others' time means arriving punctually to meetings.

definition: To organize so parts work together effectively.
vietnamese: Phối hợp; tổ chức để các bộ phận hoạt động hiệu quả cùng nhau
example: The event director coordinated efforts across multiple departments to ensure a smooth ceremony.

definition: To challenge or attack as false or questionable.
vietnamese: Bác bỏ; nghi ngờ; tấn công tính xác thực
example: The lawyer tried to impugn the witness's credibility with evidence of past dishonesty.

definition: Unnecessary or excessive.
vietnamese: Thừa thãi; không cần thiết
example: She removed superfluous details to make the report more concise.

definition: The reversal of the normal order of things.
vietnamese: Sự đảo ngược; đảo trật tự thông thường
example: The poem uses inversion of syntax to create a dramatic effect.

definition: Applying to a period in the past.
vietnamese: Hồi tố; áp dụng ngược về quá khứ
example: The new tax law was applied retroactively to income earned the previous year.

definition: To bring to an end.
vietnamese: Chấm dứt; kết thúc; đình chỉ
example: The company decided to terminate the contract due to repeated violations.

definition: To happen again or repeatedly.
vietnamese: Tái diễn; xảy ra lại; lặp lại
example: The nightmare recurred every night for a week after the traumatic event.

definition: Making someone or something look bad.
vietnamese: Không tâng bốc; làm xấu đi; không có lợi cho hình ảnh
example: The unflattering portrait drew laughter from the crowd at the gallery opening.

definition: Not exact or accurate.
vietnamese: Không chính xác; mơ hồ; thiếu độ chính xác
example: His imprecise use of language caused misunderstandings among the team.

definition: To go around or avoid something.
vietnamese: Bỏ qua; đi vòng; né tránh
example: The new highway was built to bypass the congested city center.

definition: Happening by chance, not planned.
vietnamese: Vô tình; ngẫu nhiên; không cố ý
example: The discovery of penicillin was largely accidental, made during a routine experiment.

definition: Clearly recognizable or obvious.
vietnamese: Không thể nhầm lẫn; rõ ràng dứt khoát
example: There was an unmistakable smell of smoke coming from the building.

definition: A quality or characteristic; also to credit something to a cause.
vietnamese: Đặc điểm; thuộc tính; quy cho nguyên nhân nào đó
example: Her success was attributed to years of disciplined practice and hard work.

definition: To give responsibility or care to someone.
vietnamese: Giao phó; tin tưởng trao cho ai đó trách nhiệm
example: She entrusted her most sensitive documents to her most reliable assistant.

definition: Indicated or warned of beforehand.
vietnamese: Báo trước; tiên đoán; là điềm báo của
example: The dark clouds presaged a violent storm later that evening.

definition: Not in harmony or fitting with.
vietnamese: Không hài hòa; không phù hợp; kỳ lạ so với xung quanh
example: The modern glass skyscraper looked incongruous among the ancient stone buildings.

definition: The wide range or extent of something.
vietnamese: Chiều rộng; phạm vi rộng; độ bao quát
example: The breadth of her knowledge impressed even the most senior professors at the university.

definition: Lack of interest or concern about something.
vietnamese: Sự thờ ơ; thiếu quan tâm; bàng quan
example: Voter apathy is a serious challenge for democratic participation in many countries.

definition: To teach someone to accept a set of beliefs uncritically.
vietnamese: Nhồi nhét tư tưởng; áp đặt niềm tin mà không khuyến khích phê phán
example: Critics accused the regime of indoctrinating children through state-controlled education.

definition: Unrelated or irrelevant to a subject.
vietnamese: Ngoài lề; không liên quan; không thuộc chủ đề
example: The editor removed all extraneous material that distracted from the central argument.

definition: Causing harm or damage, often in a subtle or gradual way.
vietnamese: Có hại; độc hại theo cách tinh vi hoặc từ từ
example: The pernicious effects of misinformation can take years to undo.

definition: To explain or present in detail.
vietnamese: Giải thích chi tiết; trình bày đầy đủ
example: The professor expounded her theory for over an hour to the enthralled audience.

definition: To prolong or extend in time.
vietnamese: Kéo dài; làm kéo dài hơn cần thiết
example: The negotiations were protracted over several months with no clear resolution in sight.

definition: To spread throughout or penetrate.
vietnamese: Thấm vào; lan tràn khắp; xuyên suốt
example: The smell of coffee permeated the entire office each morning.

definition: To omit or leave out, especially in speech or writing.
vietnamese: Lược bỏ; bỏ qua; không đề cập đến
example: In casual speech, people often elide certain syllables for the sake of speed.

definition: Impartial, not influenced by strong emotion.
vietnamese: Khách quan; không bị cảm xúc chi phối; vô tư lạnh lùng
example: A dispassionate analysis of the data reveals that neither side was entirely correct.

definition: Likely to change; not consistent.
vietnamese: Không ổn định; hay thay đổi; biến số
example: Spring weather is highly variable, shifting from sunshine to storms within hours.

definition: To put into effect or carry out.
vietnamese: Thực hiện; triển khai; áp dụng vào thực tế
example: The school board voted to implement a new bilingual curriculum from next year.

definition: A person who advocates or supports a cause.
vietnamese: Người ủng hộ; người đề xướng; người bênh vực
example: She has long been a proponent of universal healthcare for all citizens.

definition: To make something new again or restore.
vietnamese: Gia hạn; làm mới; phục hồi
example: They decided to renew their wedding vows on their 25th anniversary.

definition: To interpret wrongly.
vietnamese: Hiểu sai; diễn giải nhầm
example: His words were misconstrued by the press as a threat rather than a warning.

definition: Present everywhere at the same time.
vietnamese: Có mặt khắp nơi; phổ biến rộng rãi
example: Mobile phones have become ubiquitous in both developed and developing nations.

definition: To make something worse by adding to it; also a mixture of elements.
vietnamese: Làm phức tạp thêm; hợp chất; kết hợp nhiều yếu tố
example: Missing several deadlines compounded the team's existing reputation problems.

definition: To hinder or obstruct progress.
vietnamese: Cản trở; ngăn chặn tiến độ
example: Budget cuts stymied the research team's efforts to expand the study.

definition: Dependent on certain conditions or events.
vietnamese: Phụ thuộc vào điều kiện nhất định; có điều kiện
example: The job offer was contingent on the successful completion of a background check.

definition: Required or compelled to do something.
vietnamese: Có nghĩa vụ; bị bắt buộc phải làm điều gì đó
example: As a witness, she felt morally obligated to tell the truth in court.

definition: Held back or restrained by something.
vietnamese: Bị kìm hãm; bị ức chế; bị kiềm chế
example: She felt inhibited in large social gatherings but thrived in one-on-one conversations.

definition: Unable to be touched or physically measured; abstract.
vietnamese: Vô hình; không thể đo lường được; trừu tượng
example: The company's brand loyalty is an intangible asset of enormous value.

definition: Able to be explained.
vietnamese: Có thể giải thích được; dễ hiểu
example: His sudden change of mood was explicable once we learned what had happened.

definition: With deep respect or awe.
vietnamese: Một cách tôn kính; với lòng kính sợ sâu sắc
example: The students listened reverentially as the Nobel laureate spoke about her discoveries.

definition: Working together with others.
vietnamese: Sự hợp tác; làm việc cùng nhau hướng đến mục tiêu chung
example: The film was the result of a long collaboration between the director and composer.

definition: To go beyond one's limits or authority.
vietnamese: Vượt quá giới hạn; làm quá mức thẩm quyền
example: The executive overreached his authority by signing contracts without board approval.

definition: To overshadow or surpass.
vietnamese: Che khuất; vượt qua; làm mờ nhạt điều khác
example: Her second novel eclipsed the success of her debut in both sales and critical acclaim.

definition: False or not genuine.
vietnamese: Giả mạo; không xác thực; thiếu căn cứ
example: The court dismissed the spurious claims as having no factual basis whatsoever.

definition: Required or mandatory.
vietnamese: Bắt buộc; cưỡng chế; không được từ chối
example: Attendance at the safety briefing is compulsory for all new employees.

definition: Calm and peaceful.
vietnamese: Yên tĩnh; bình thản; thanh bình
example: The tranquil lake reflected the mountains perfectly on the still morning.

definition: A temporary relief from punishment or difficulty.
vietnamese: Sự giảm nhẹ tạm thời; hoãn hình phạt; thở phào nhẹ nhõm
example: The unexpected rain gave the exhausted hikers a welcome reprieve from the scorching heat.

definition: To suggest without stating directly.
vietnamese: Ám chỉ; gợi ý; ngụ ý mà không nói thẳng
example: Her silence implied that she was not happy with the decision.

definition: Not leading to a firm conclusion.
