vietnamese: Không dứt khoát; chưa có kết luận rõ ràng
example: The results of the study were inconclusive, requiring further investigation.

definition: To come together to form one whole.
vietnamese: Hợp lại; kết hợp thành một khối thống nhất
example: Small community groups coalesced into a powerful movement for local change.

definition: To make into law or put into action.
vietnamese: Ban hành; thực thi; đưa vào luật hoặc hành động
example: Congress enacted new legislation to regulate the use of social media platforms.

definition: Lacking order or planning; random.
vietnamese: Lộn xộn; thiếu tổ chức; ngẫu nhiên không có kế hoạch
example: The files were stored in a haphazard manner, making it nearly impossible to find anything.

definition: To replace one thing with another.
vietnamese: Thay thế; dùng thứ này thay cho thứ khác
example: You can substitute honey for sugar in many baking recipes.

definition: A summary or conclusion.
vietnamese: Tóm tắt; kết luận; tổng kết
example: The lawyer's summation brilliantly reviewed all key evidence for the jury.

definition: Relating to what did not happen; contrary to facts.
vietnamese: Phản thực tế; giả thiết điều không xảy ra
example: Historians often engage in counterfactual reasoning to understand pivotal historical decisions.

definition: Based on analogy or comparison.
vietnamese: Dựa trên sự tương đồng; mang tính so sánh
example: The teacher used analogical reasoning to help students understand abstract concepts.

definition: Connected in the mind by relation or experience.
vietnamese: Liên kết; mang tính liên tưởng; kết nối qua kinh nghiệm
example: The smell of cinnamon triggered associative memories of childhood holidays.

definition: An earlier event or decision that serves as a guide.
vietnamese: Tiền lệ; sự kiện hoặc quyết định trước làm cơ sở tham khảo
example: The landmark court ruling set a precedent for future cases involving digital privacy.

definition: To assume beforehand.
vietnamese: Giả định trước; tiền đề cho rằng điều gì đó đã đúng
example: The question presupposes that all students have equal access to technology.

definition: Existing as an idea or concept, not reality.
vietnamese: Chỉ tồn tại trong ý niệm; mang tính lý thuyết; không thực tế
example: The proposed budget was based on notional figures that had never been verified.

definition: Used to emphasize truth or accuracy.
vietnamese: Thực sự; đúng là; dùng để nhấn mạnh sự thật
example: The new shopping mall was a veritable labyrinth of corridors and escalators.

definition: Serious and sincere.
vietnamese: Nghiêm túc; thành tâm; chân thành
example: She made an earnest effort to understand his perspective before responding.

definition: Having a dislike or distrust of humankind.
vietnamese: Ghét con người; không tin tưởng nhân loại
example: His misanthropic outlook led him to avoid social gatherings whenever possible.

definition: To detach or separate.
vietnamese: Rút ra; tách rời; ngắt kết nối
example: It can be difficult to disengage from social media once you become accustomed to it.

definition: To set the boundaries or limits of something.
vietnamese: Phân định ranh giới; vạch giới hạn
example: The treaty clearly demarcated the border between the two nations.

definition: To combine two or more things into one.
vietnamese: Nhập lẫn; kết hợp hai điều khác nhau thành một
example: Critics accused the journalist of conflating correlation with causation in the report.

definition: The act of hiding something.
vietnamese: Sự che giấu; hành động ẩn giấu điều gì đó
example: The concealment of financial records was considered obstruction of justice.

definition: To make something look or feel younger or fresher.
vietnamese: Làm trẻ hóa; phục hồi sức sống; làm tươi mới
example: A week of rest and good food was enough to rejuvenate the exhausted traveler.

definition: To find a solution or make a firm decision.
vietnamese: Giải quyết; quyết tâm; tìm ra giải pháp dứt khoát
example: The two countries resolved their territorial dispute through peaceful negotiation.

definition: Impossible to achieve or reach.
vietnamese: Không thể đạt được; ngoài tầm với
example: For many families, owning a home in the city has become virtually unattainable.

definition: To make firm or stronger.
vietnamese: Củng cố; làm vững chắc; đông cứng lại
example: Winning the championship helped solidify the team's reputation as the best in the league.

definition: Enthusiastic and public praise.
vietnamese: Hoan hô; lời khen ngợi công khai và nhiệt liệt
example: The novel received widespread acclaim from critics and readers around the world.

definition: Failure to fulfill an obligation; standard or automatic option.
vietnamese: Vỡ nợ; tùy chọn mặc định; không hoàn thành nghĩa vụ
example: The software opens in dark mode by default unless the user changes the settings.

definition: Official permission or approval; or a penalty for disobedience.
vietnamese: Chấp thuận chính thức; lệnh trừng phạt; hình phạt vì vi phạm
example: Trade sanctions were imposed on the country after it violated international law.

definition: Something that does not fit or is out of place.
vietnamese: Sự không ăn khớp; điều không phù hợp; mâu thuẫn
example: The incongruity of wearing a tuxedo at a beach party drew many stares.

definition: To intervene on behalf of someone.
vietnamese: Can thiệp; làm trung gian bảo hộ ai đó
example: She interceded on behalf of the junior employee who had been unfairly reprimanded.

definition: To agree with.
vietnamese: Đồng ý; tán thành; có cùng quan điểm
example: All committee members concurred that the proposal needed significant revision.

definition: To gather or build up gradually.
vietnamese: Tích lũy; dần dần thu gom; chất chồng theo thời gian
example: Dust accumulates quickly in rooms that are rarely cleaned.

definition: To separate from others.
vietnamese: Cô lập; tách biệt; phân cách khỏi môi trường xung quanh
example: The patient was isolated to prevent the contagious illness from spreading further.

definition: To obtain or gain possession of.
vietnamese: Thu thập; đạt được; có được quyền sở hữu
example: She acquired fluency in three languages through years of study and immersion.

definition: Complexity or refinement.
vietnamese: Sự tinh tế; phức tạp; trình độ cao về văn hóa hoặc kỹ thuật
example: The sophistication of the design impressed even experienced engineers.

definition: To imagine or picture in the mind.
vietnamese: Hình dung; tưởng tượng; hình dung tương lai
example: The architect envisioned a building that would blend seamlessly into the surrounding landscape.

definition: Gradually decreasing.
vietnamese: Giảm dần; thu hẹp; ngày càng ít đi
example: The dwindling population of monarch butterflies alarmed conservation scientists.

definition: Acting in one's own interest.
vietnamese: Vì lợi ích cá nhân; hành động để phục vụ bản thân
example: He self-servingly interpreted the contract in a way that benefited only himself.

definition: Reluctantly or unwillingly.
vietnamese: Miễn cưỡng; bất đắc dĩ; không vui lòng nhưng vẫn làm
example: He grudgingly admitted that his rival had performed better in the competition.

definition: Disapproval or dislike.
vietnamese: Sự không tán thành; ghét bỏ; không được ưa
example: The controversial proposal fell into disfavor after details of its costs were revealed.

definition: To present something as less important than it is.
vietnamese: Nói nhẹ đi; đánh giá thấp; trình bày ít hơn thực tế
example: To say the project was challenging would be to understate the enormous difficulties involved.

definition: Dishonest or deceitful.
vietnamese: Gian lận; lừa đảo; không trung thực
example: The company was shut down after fraudulent accounting practices were uncovered.

definition: Overstepping proper bounds; arrogant.
vietnamese: Kiêu ngạo; lạm quyền; vượt quá giới hạn thích hợp
example: It was presumptuous of him to assume he would be invited to lead the project.

definition: To give as a gift or honor.
vietnamese: Ban tặng; trao tặng; phong tặng như một vinh dự
example: The university bestowed an honorary degree upon the celebrated humanitarian.

definition: To distribute or assign resources or duties.
vietnamese: Phân bổ; phân phối; giao nhiệm vụ hoặc tài nguyên
example: The government allocated a large portion of the budget to healthcare and education.

definition: To be a perfect example of something.
vietnamese: Là hình mẫu tiêu biểu; là điển hình của điều gì đó
example: Her dedication to students epitomizes the spirit of truly great teaching.

definition: To represent something as greater than it is.
vietnamese: Phóng đại; cường điệu; trình bày quá mức thực tế
example: News headlines often exaggerate minor events to attract more readers.

definition: To take advantage of an opportunity.
vietnamese: Tận dụng cơ hội; khai thác lợi thế; viết hoa
example: The startup capitalized on the surge in demand for remote working tools.

definition: To harm the reputation of; to show as untrue.
vietnamese: Làm mất uy tín; bác bỏ; chứng minh là sai
example: The defendant's lawyer attempted to discredit the prosecution's key witness.

definition: To get something back.
vietnamese: Lấy lại; khôi phục; tìm và mang về
example: She retrieved the deleted files using a data recovery program.

definition: To combine or bring together.
vietnamese: Tích hợp; kết hợp; đưa vào một hệ thống thống nhất
example: The new software integrates seamlessly with all existing company tools.

definition: To keep safe from harm or decay.
vietnamese: Bảo tồn; gìn giữ; ngăn không cho hư hại
example: Efforts are underway to preserve the ancient manuscripts from further deterioration.

definition: To justify or explain logically.
vietnamese: Hợp lý hóa; biện hộ; giải thích theo logic
example: He rationalized his decision by focusing only on the potential benefits.

definition: Idealistic and impractical.
vietnamese: Không tưởng; lý tưởng nhưng không thực tế
example: Critics dismissed the policy as utopian, arguing it ignored real-world constraints.

definition: Intended as punishment.
vietnamese: Mang tính trừng phạt; nhằm mục đích trừng trị
example: The punitive damages awarded by the court sent a clear message to the industry.

definition: To postpone or delay.
vietnamese: Trì hoãn; hoãn lại; để sau
example: They decided to defer the major decision until more information was available.

definition: To take the place of something or someone.
vietnamese: Thay thế; dịch chuyển; chiếm chỗ của người hoặc vật khác
example: Automation has displaced many workers in the manufacturing sector.

definition: Able to be exchanged or replaced.
vietnamese: Có thể hoán đổi; thay thế lẫn nhau một cách dễ dàng
example: The two components are interchangeable and can be used in either position.

definition: Lower in rank or position.
vietnamese: Cấp dưới; thứ yếu; thuộc vị trí thấp hơn
example: A good leader respects the ideas of subordinates rather than dismissing them.

definition: To cause or accelerate a reaction or change.
vietnamese: Xúc tác; thúc đẩy; khởi phát sự thay đổi
example: The viral video catalyzed a nationwide conversation about racial equality.

definition: Lacking movement or change.
vietnamese: Tĩnh; không thay đổi; cố định
example: In a rapidly changing market, a static strategy is a recipe for failure.

definition: Excessive or extreme.
vietnamese: Quá mức; cực đoan; không điều độ
example: His immoderate consumption of sugar led to serious health problems later in life.

definition: To press or squeeze together.
vietnamese: Nén lại; ép chặt; thu gọn
example: The editor compressed the 10-minute speech into a 30-second highlight clip.

definition: To base something on.
vietnamese: Dựa vào; lấy làm nền tảng; khẳng định về
example: The entire business model was predicated on the assumption of continued growth.

definition: To successfully deal with or defeat.
vietnamese: Vượt qua; khắc phục; chiến thắng khó khăn
example: With persistence and support, she was able to overcome her fear of public speaking.

definition: To force or drive.
vietnamese: Thúc ép; bắt buộc; thôi thúc mạnh mẽ
example: The new evidence compelled the prosecutor to reconsider the charges.

definition: Fairness; lack of bias.
vietnamese: Sự công bằng; không thiên vị; trung lập
example: The commission was praised for its impartiality in handling the sensitive investigation.

definition: Of considerable importance or size.
vietnamese: Đáng kể; lớn về quy mô hoặc tầm quan trọng
example: The new investment brought a substantial improvement in the company's financial position.

definition: To stop the continuous progress of something.
vietnamese: Gián đoạn; ngắt quãng; làm gián đoạn tiến trình
example: A sudden power outage interrupted the live broadcast at the most critical moment.

definition: To go beyond a limit.
vietnamese: Vượt quá; cao hơn; vượt giới hạn
example: Her performance far exceeded even the most optimistic expectations.

definition: To place in a category or group.
vietnamese: Phân loại; xếp vào nhóm; phân nhóm
example: It is difficult to categorize his music since it blends so many different genres.

definition: A disturbance or disruption.
vietnamese: Sự xáo trộn; nhiễu loạn; bất ổn
example: Even a small perturbation in the system caused significant downstream effects.

definition: Avoidance of activity or exertion; laziness.
vietnamese: Sự lười biếng; không muốn gắng sức; thụ động
example: His indolence was the main reason he failed to complete the project on time.

definition: Reduction or decrease.
vietnamese: Sự giảm bớt; giảm thiểu; suy giảm
example: The noise abatement policy restricted construction work during nighttime hours.

definition: A collection or gathering of things.
vietnamese: Sự tập hợp; sự gom góp; tổng hợp từ nhiều nguồn
example: The aggregation of small donations added up to a significant total.

definition: To take away or eliminate.
vietnamese: Loại bỏ; xóa đi; lấy đi
example: The new policy aimed to remove all unnecessary bureaucratic procedures.

definition: To open or reveal something gradually.
vietnamese: Mở ra; dần dần được hé lộ; diễn ra từng bước
example: The full extent of the scandal only unfolded over the course of several months.

definition: To change or alter.
vietnamese: Thay đổi; biến đổi; khác đi
example: Prices vary significantly depending on the season and availability of materials.

definition: To assess or determine the size, amount, or degree of something.
vietnamese: Đo lường; đánh giá; xác định kích thước hoặc mức độ
example: Success should not be measured solely by financial gain.

definition: Happening by chance without apparent cause.
vietnamese: Trùng hợp ngẫu nhiên; không có nguyên nhân rõ ràng
example: Their meeting at the airport was purely coincidental — neither had known the other would be there.

definition: To twist out of shape or misrepresent.
vietnamese: Bóp méo; làm biến dạng; xuyên tạc sự thật
example: The politician accused the reporter of distorting his statements out of context.

definition: Failure to care for properly.
vietnamese: Xao lãng; bỏ bê; không chú ý chăm sóc đúng mức
example: Neglect of basic maintenance caused the building to deteriorate rapidly.

definition: Something added to complete or enhance.
vietnamese: Bổ sung; thêm vào để hoàn chỉnh hoặc nâng cao
example: She took vitamin D supplements to compensate for the lack of sunlight in winter.

definition: To cause or bring about.
vietnamese: Kích thích; gây ra; thúc đẩy điều gì đó xảy ra
example: The medication can induce drowsiness, so patients should avoid driving after taking it.

definition: To separate or disengage.
vietnamese: Tách biệt; giải phóng khỏi sự phụ thuộc lẫn nhau
example: Economists are exploring ways to decouple economic growth from environmental damage.

definition: The way something is understood or interpreted.
vietnamese: Nhận thức; sự nhận biết; cách hiểu và diễn giải
example: Public perception of the company improved dramatically after the charity campaign.

definition: To recognize or establish as being.
vietnamese: Nhận diện; xác định; xác nhận là ai hoặc cái gì
example: Researchers identified several key factors contributing to the rise in chronic illness.

definition: To make known or show.
vietnamese: Tiết lộ; hé lộ; cho thấy điều trước đây ẩn giấu
example: The audit revealed numerous irregularities in the organization's financial records.

definition: The ability to return to original shape after stretching; flexibility.
vietnamese: Tính đàn hồi; khả năng phục hồi; sự linh hoạt
example: The elasticity of the material allowed it to be stretched without tearing.

definition: A feeling of fondness or love.
vietnamese: Tình cảm; lòng yêu thương; sự quý mến
example: The children showed great affection toward the retired teacher who had mentored them.

definition: Justified or deserved.
vietnamese: Có lý do chính đáng; xứng đáng; được biện hộ
example: Given the evidence, her concerns about safety were fully warranted.

definition: The state of being unknown or unclear.
vietnamese: Sự vô danh; sự tối nghĩa; trạng thái không được biết đến
example: The talented musician spent years in obscurity before being discovered.

definition: Lack of depth, either literal or figurative.
vietnamese: Sự cạn cợt; thiếu chiều sâu; hời hợt
example: Critics pointed to the shallowness of the film's characters as its main weakness.

definition: Characterized by constant change or activity.
vietnamese: Năng động; đầy sức sống; không ngừng thay đổi
example: A dynamic work environment encourages creativity and adaptability.

definition: Able to be maintained over time without depletion.
vietnamese: Bền vững; có thể duy trì mà không làm cạn kiệt nguồn lực
example: The company shifted to sustainable packaging to reduce its environmental footprint.

definition: Using irony to mock or convey contempt.
vietnamese: Mỉa mai; châm biếm; dùng lời nói bóng gió để chế giễu
example: Her sarcastic remark about his 'brilliant idea' made it clear she disagreed.

definition: Old-fashioned or obsolete.
vietnamese: Lỗi thời; lạc hậu; không còn phù hợp nữa
example: The outdated software was unable to support the new operating system's features.

definition: A firm decision or solution to a problem.
vietnamese: Nghị quyết; quyết tâm; giải pháp dứt khoát
example: The UN passed a resolution calling for an immediate ceasefire.

definition: The reason or explanation for something.
vietnamese: Sự biện hộ; lý do chính đáng; lời giải thích
example: There was no justification for the aggressive response to a peaceful protest.

definition: Discussion aimed at reaching an agreement.
vietnamese: Đàm phán; thương lượng; thảo luận để đi đến thỏa thuận
example: Months of negotiation finally produced a trade deal acceptable to both nations.

definition: Unwillingly or hesitantly.
vietnamese: Miễn cưỡng; không sẵn lòng; do dự khi hành động
example: He reluctantly agreed to take on the additional responsibilities.

definition: Feeling sentimental longing for the past.
vietnamese: Hoài niệm; nhớ về quá khứ với tình cảm trân trọng
example: Old photographs always made her feel nostalgic for her childhood summers.

definition: Causing strong reaction or stimulating thought.
vietnamese: Khiêu khích; gây phản ứng mạnh; kích thích suy nghĩ
example: The artist was known for provocative installations that challenged social norms.

definition: To combine or unite into one.
vietnamese: Sáp nhập; hợp nhất; kết hợp thành một thể
example: Several smaller agencies amalgamated to form one powerful regulatory body.

definition: To officially announce or make known.
vietnamese: Ban bố; công bố rộng rãi; tuyên truyền chính thức
example: The king promulgated a new law granting greater freedom of the press.

definition: To combine into a more effective or coherent whole.
vietnamese: Củng cố; sáp nhập; làm vững mạnh hơn bằng cách gộp lại
example: The merger was intended to consolidate resources and reduce operational costs.

definition: To openly resist or refuse to obey.
vietnamese: Thách thức; bất chấp; công khai không tuân theo
example: She defied expectations by starting her own business at age sixty.

definition: To predict or forecast.
vietnamese: Tiên đoán; dự báo; nói trước điều sẽ xảy ra
example: No economic model can perfectly foretell the timing of market crashes.

definition: Not based on fact or evidence.
vietnamese: Không có căn cứ; không dựa trên sự thật hoặc bằng chứng
example: The rumors about the company's collapse were entirely unfounded.

definition: Possible to do easily or conveniently.
vietnamese: Khả thi; có thể thực hiện một cách thuận tiện
example: Given the timeline, it is not feasible to complete the entire project before the deadline.

definition: The ability to make decisions quickly and firmly.
vietnamese: Sự quyết đoán; khả năng ra quyết định nhanh và dứt khoát
example: Decisiveness under pressure is one of the most valuable traits a leader can have.

definition: To focus all attention or effort on something.
vietnamese: Tập trung; dồn toàn bộ sự chú ý hoặc nỗ lực vào điều gì đó
example: Turn off your phone if you want to concentrate while studying.

definition: To publicly denounce or criticize.
vietnamese: Lên án công khai; phê phán mạnh mẽ trước công chúng
example: Activists decried the factory's pollution as unacceptable in a modern society.

definition: Treating serious issues with inappropriate humor.
vietnamese: Giỡn cợt không đúng lúc; xem nhẹ vấn đề nghiêm túc bằng hài hước
example: His facetious comment during the memorial service offended many attendees.

definition: Resistance to change in motion or condition.
vietnamese: Quán tính; sức ì; kháng cự thay đổi
example: The inertia of the bureaucracy made it nearly impossible to push through reforms.

definition: The quality of being convenient and practical despite possibly being improper.
vietnamese: Tính tiện lợi thực tế dù có thể không hoàn toàn đúng đắn về mặt đạo đức
example: The decision was driven by political expediency rather than genuine concern.

definition: Fertility or ability to produce abundantly.
vietnamese: Khả năng sinh sản dồi dào; sức sản xuất phong phú
example: The poet was celebrated for the fecundity of his imagination.

definition: To speed up or facilitate.
vietnamese: Đẩy nhanh tiến độ; xúc tiến; tạo thuận lợi
example: The government introduced new measures to expedite the processing of asylum claims.

definition: A person learning a trade from a skilled employer.
vietnamese: Người học nghề; thực tập sinh đang học từ người có kinh nghiệm
example: He began his career as an apprentice under a master carpenter.

definition: To organize so parts work together effectively.
vietnamese: Phối hợp; tổ chức để các bộ phận hoạt động hiệu quả
example: The team leader coordinated efforts across three different departments.

definition: Impossible to explain or understand.
vietnamese: Không thể giải thích; khó hiểu một cách kỳ lạ
example: There was an inexplicable chill in the room despite the warm temperature outside.

definition: Showing concern for others' feelings.
vietnamese: Chu đáo; biết nghĩ đến cảm xúc của người khác
example: A considerate neighbor keeps noise to a minimum during late-night hours.

definition: A disturbance or disruption.
vietnamese: Sự nhiễu loạn; bất ổn; xáo trộn hệ thống
example: Gravitational perturbations caused the satellite to drift slightly off course.

definition: Careful watchfulness for danger or problems.
vietnamese: Sự cảnh giác cao độ; theo dõi cẩn thận để phát hiện nguy hiểm
example: Airport security relies on constant vigilance to prevent potential threats.

definition: Careful consideration or discussion before making a decision.
vietnamese: Thảo luận kỹ; suy xét thấu đáo trước khi quyết định
example: The board entered three days of deliberation before announcing its choice.

definition: Having many different parts or forms.
vietnamese: Đa dạng; nhiều hình thức; phức tạp với nhiều yếu tố khác nhau
example: The multifarious demands of the job left him little time for personal pursuits.

definition: Unable to express ideas clearly.
vietnamese: Không diễn đạt rõ ràng; lúng túng khi nói; thiếu khả năng biểu đạt
example: He was usually articulate but became inarticulate under intense pressure.

definition: The act of avoiding something, especially by trickery.
vietnamese: Sự né tránh; lẩn tránh trách nhiệm bằng thủ thuật
example: His evasion of direct questions made investigators increasingly suspicious.

definition: Wrongly interpreted or judged.
vietnamese: Bị hiểu sai; bị đánh giá nhầm
example: The policy was widely misunderstood because it was poorly communicated to the public.

definition: To cause someone to feel ashamed or embarrassed.
vietnamese: Làm nhục; gây cảm giác xấu hổ hoặc bối rối cho ai đó
example: It is wrong to humiliate someone publicly for making an honest mistake.

definition: The act of allowing entry or confessing something.
vietnamese: Sự thừa nhận; cho phép vào; lời thú nhận
example: His admission of guilt surprised even his closest allies.

definition: Dependent on something or someone.
vietnamese: Phụ thuộc vào; dựa dẫm vào ai đó hoặc điều gì đó
example: The remote village was entirely reliant on a single road for supplies.

definition: The quality of being easily affected or responsive.
vietnamese: Độ nhạy cảm; dễ bị tác động; khả năng phản ứng
example: Sensitivity to others' needs is a crucial quality for anyone in a caregiving role.

definition: Praised or expressed approval of someone or something.
vietnamese: Được khen ngợi; được bày tỏ sự tán thưởng
example: The chef was complimented by every guest at the dinner for the exceptional meal.

definition: Acting without care for consequences.
vietnamese: Liều lĩnh; bất cẩn; không quan tâm đến hậu quả
example: Reckless driving endangers not only the driver but everyone else on the road.

definition: To change the position of two things.
vietnamese: Hoán vị; đổi chỗ; chuyển từ vị trí này sang vị trí khác
example: Accidentally transposing two digits caused a significant error in the bank transfer.

definition: A deviation from what is normal or expected.
vietnamese: Sai lệch; lệch chuẩn; điều bất thường so với thông thường
example: Scientists considered the unusual weather pattern an aberration rather than a new trend.

definition: To obtain something by force or threats.
vietnamese: Tống tiền; cưỡng đoạt; dùng áp lực để lấy điều gì đó
example: The gang was arrested for attempting to extort money from local business owners.

definition: To bring under control, often by force.
vietnamese: Khuất phục; kiềm chế; đưa vào tầm kiểm soát
example: Officers worked to subdue the situation before it escalated into violence.

definition: Relating to formal events or rituals.
vietnamese: Mang tính nghi lễ; theo nghi thức trang trọng
example: The flag was ceremonially raised at dawn to mark the beginning of independence celebrations.

definition: To put an end to or prevent something.
vietnamese: Đàn áp; ngăn chặn; kìm hãm
example: The regime sought to suppress any criticism of its policies through censorship.

definition: Peculiar to an individual; distinctive.
vietnamese: Đặc trưng cá nhân; mang nét kỳ quặc riêng biệt
example: His idiosyncratic writing style set his novels apart from all other contemporary fiction.

definition: Impossible to separate or escape from.
vietnamese: Không thể tách rời; không thể giải thoát
example: Culture and identity are inextricably linked.

definition: No longer in use; outdated.
vietnamese: Lỗi thời; đã lạc hậu; không còn được dùng nữa
example: Cassette tapes became obsolete after the introduction of CDs and digital music.

definition: Critical observation or examination.
vietnamese: Sự xem xét kỹ lưỡng; kiểm tra nghiêm ngặt; giám sát chặt chẽ
example: The government's spending plans came under intense scrutiny from opposition lawmakers.

definition: To increase rapidly in number.
vietnamese: Sinh sôi nảy nở; gia tăng nhanh chóng về số lượng
example: Social media platforms have proliferated to the point where most people use several simultaneously.

definition: Showing great attention to detail.
vietnamese: Tỉ mỉ; cẩn thận từng chi tiết
example: Her meticulous planning ensured the event went smoothly without a single problem.

definition: Inborn; natural; not learned.
vietnamese: Bẩm sinh; tự nhiên; không cần học
example: Some researchers believe that certain moral instincts are innate rather than taught.

definition: To accept or allow behavior considered wrong.
vietnamese: Dung túng; cho phép điều được coi là sai
example: The management refused to condone bullying in any form in the workplace.

definition: Expressed clearly; easy to understand.
vietnamese: Rõ ràng; dễ hiểu; sáng suốt
example: Her lucid explanation made even the most complex topic accessible to beginners.

definition: Dealing with things sensibly and realistically.
vietnamese: Thực dụng; thiết thực; tiếp cận vấn đề một cách thực tế
example: A pragmatic approach to the crisis focused on what could realistically be achieved.

definition: Holding firmly to something; persistent.
vietnamese: Kiên trì; bám chặt; bền bỉ không từ bỏ
example: Her tenacious pursuit of justice kept the case alive for over a decade.

definition: Having mixed feelings or contradictory ideas.
vietnamese: Cảm xúc lưỡng lự; mâu thuẫn trong suy nghĩ hoặc cảm xúc
example: His ambivalence about the job offer made it hard for him to give a clear answer.

definition: Not flowing or moving; showing no activity.
vietnamese: Đình trệ; trì trệ; không tiến triển
example: The economy remained stagnant for years despite repeated government interventions.

definition: Gradually destroying something; damaging over time.
vietnamese: Ăn mòn; phá hủy dần dần; có hại lâu dài
example: The corrosive effects of cynicism can slowly erode team morale.

definition: To grow or develop in a healthy way; to thrive.
vietnamese: Phát triển rực rỡ; hưng thịnh; phát triển mạnh mẽ
example: Small businesses flourish when they receive adequate support from local governments.

definition: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work.
vietnamese: Chăm chỉ; cần mẫn; tận tâm trong công việc
example: The diligent student spent hours reviewing her notes before every exam.

definition: To be a typical example of something.
vietnamese: Minh họa; là ví dụ điển hình; tiêu biểu cho điều gì đó
example: Her career exemplifies the determination needed to succeed in a competitive field.

definition: Tending to cause harm or damage.
vietnamese: Có hại; gây tổn hại; bất lợi
example: A lack of sleep is detrimental to both cognitive performance and physical health.

definition: To accept or admit the existence of something.
vietnamese: Thừa nhận; công nhận; chấp nhận sự tồn tại của điều gì đó
example: It takes courage to acknowledge one's own mistakes openly and without excuses.

definition: Having subtle distinctions or slight variations.
vietnamese: Có sắc thái; tinh tế; thể hiện sự khác biệt nhỏ nhưng quan trọng
example: A nuanced reading of the text reveals layers of meaning not apparent at first glance.

definition: More important than anything else; supreme.
vietnamese: Tối quan trọng; hàng đầu; vượt trội hơn tất cả
example: Patient safety must be paramount in every decision made by healthcare providers.

definition: Open to more than one interpretation.
vietnamese: Mơ hồ; không rõ ràng; có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách
example: The contract's ambiguous language led to a prolonged legal dispute.

definition: An opinion without sufficient evidence.
vietnamese: Phỏng đoán; suy đoán thiếu bằng chứng
example: Without more data, scientists could only conjecture about the cause of the anomaly.

definition: To lessen the gravity or severity of something.
vietnamese: Giảm nhẹ mức độ nghiêm trọng; xoa dịu
example: Planting trees in urban areas can help mitigate the effects of air pollution.

definition: Rapid increase in number or amount.
vietnamese: Sự gia tăng nhanh chóng; sự phổ biến rộng rãi
example: The proliferation of misinformation online poses a major challenge for journalists.

definition: To examine closely and thoroughly.
vietnamese: Xem xét kỹ lưỡng; kiểm tra chi tiết; phân tích tỉ mỉ
example: Every clause in the agreement was scrutinized by a team of expert lawyers.

definition: To make something less severe; to relieve.
vietnamese: Làm giảm bớt; xoa dịu; giảm nhẹ gánh nặng hoặc đau khổ
example: New pain-management techniques have helped alleviate suffering for millions of patients.

definition: Logical and well-organized; easy to understand.
vietnamese: Mạch lạc; nhất quán; rõ ràng và có tổ chức
example: A coherent argument is built on clear logic and well-supported evidence.

definition: To cause something to happen suddenly or prematurely.
vietnamese: Gây ra đột ngột; đẩy nhanh; làm xảy ra sớm hơn dự kiến
example: The announcement precipitated a sharp drop in the company's stock price.

definition: So small or unimportant as to be not worth considering.
vietnamese: Không đáng kể; quá nhỏ để quan tâm; tầm thường
example: The additional cost was negligible compared to the benefits the upgrade would bring.

definition: To spread or promote widely.
vietnamese: Truyền bá; lan truyền; phổ biến rộng rãi
example: Social media can rapidly propagate both accurate information and dangerous myths.

definition: Based on random choice rather than reason or system.
vietnamese: Tùy tiện; ngẫu hứng; không dựa trên lý do hay hệ thống
example: The rules seemed arbitrary, with no clear logic behind them.

definition: Too many to be counted; countless.
vietnamese: Vô số; không thể đếm hết; cực kỳ nhiều
example: She had made innumerable attempts to contact the office without success.

definition: Not securely held; dangerously likely to fall.
vietnamese: Bấp bênh; không ổn định; dễ sụp đổ hoặc nguy hiểm
example: The workers balanced on a precarious scaffold several stories above the ground.

definition: To admit something is true after resisting it.
vietnamese: Thừa nhận; chịu thua; chấp nhận sau khi phản đối
example: After reviewing the data, the scientist was forced to concede that his hypothesis was wrong.

definition: Deserving censure or condemnation.
vietnamese: Đáng trách; đáng lên án; không thể chấp nhận về mặt đạo đức
example: The board declared the executive's behavior reprehensible and called for his immediate resignation.

definition: Slightly connected or diverging from the main topic.
vietnamese: Tiếp tuyến; hơi liên quan; lệch khỏi chủ đề chính
example: The speaker's tangential comments distracted from the main point of the lecture.

definition: Having mixed feelings about something.
vietnamese: Lưỡng lự; có cảm xúc trái chiều; không dứt khoát
example: Many voters felt ambivalent about both candidates in the election.

definition: Having a strong desire for revenge.
vietnamese: Thù hận; hay trả thù; mang lòng oán giận muốn báo thù
example: His vindictive behavior after the breakup alarmed mutual friends.

definition: Showing selfless concern for others.
vietnamese: Vị tha; quan tâm đến người khác hơn bản thân; không ích kỷ
example: Her altruistic decision to donate her prize money to charity moved the entire audience.

definition: Featuring new methods or ideas.
vietnamese: Sáng tạo; đổi mới; đưa ra phương pháp hoặc ý tưởng mới
example: The innovative design won first prize at the international engineering competition.

definition: Giving rise to public disagreement.
vietnamese: Gây tranh cãi; gây ra ý kiến trái chiều trong công chúng
example: The book was controversial when it was first published but is now considered a classic.

definition: Showing great attention to detail.
vietnamese: Tỉ mỉ; cẩn thận; chú ý từng chi tiết nhỏ
example: The restoration team was meticulous in recreating every original detail of the artwork.

definition: Curious; eager to learn or know.
vietnamese: Tò mò; ham tìm hiểu; thích khám phá điều mới
example: An inquisitive mind is a scientist's greatest asset.

definition: A practical approach to problems and affairs.
vietnamese: Chủ nghĩa thực dụng; cách tiếp cận thực tế
example: His pragmatism allowed him to find workable solutions where others saw only obstacles.

definition: Never done or known before.
vietnamese: Chưa từng có tiền lệ; chưa từng xảy ra trước đây
example: The athlete achieved an unprecedented fourth consecutive gold medal at the Olympics.

definition: Gentle and kindly; not harmful.
vietnamese: Lành tính; vô hại; nhẹ nhàng và tốt bụng
example: The doctor confirmed the tumor was benign and did not require immediate treatment.

definition: Essentially different in kind; not able to be compared.
vietnamese: Hoàn toàn khác nhau; không thể so sánh; khác biệt về bản chất
example: The report attempted to unify disparate findings from dozens of independent studies.

definition: Never ending; continuing without pause.
vietnamese: Không ngừng nghỉ; liên tục không dứt
example: The incessant noise from the construction site made it impossible to sleep.

definition: Of crucial importance in relation to the development of something.
vietnamese: Quan trọng then chốt; mang tính bước ngoặt
example: The invention of the printing press was pivotal in spreading literacy across Europe.

definition: Lasting only for a short time; temporary.
vietnamese: Tạm thời; nhất thời; không tồn tại lâu
example: The joy of revenge is transient, while the damage it causes can be permanent.

definition: To use ambiguous language to avoid making a commitment.
vietnamese: Nói nước đôi; dùng ngôn ngữ mơ hồ để tránh cam kết
example: When asked directly, the politician continued to equivocate rather than giving a clear answer.

definition: Lasting for a very short time.
vietnamese: Phù du; thoáng qua; tồn tại rất ngắn
example: Fame can be ephemeral — today's celebrity is quickly forgotten tomorrow.

definition: Very attentive to accuracy and detail; difficult to please.
vietnamese: Kỹ lưỡng; khó tính; chú ý đến từng chi tiết nhỏ nhất
example: The fastidious editor caught errors that every other reader had missed.

definition: Close, careful examination.
vietnamese: Sự xem xét kỹ lưỡng; kiểm tra chặt chẽ
example: The new policy underwent intense scrutiny from members of the opposition party.

definition: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers.
vietnamese: Cảnh giác; đề phòng; thận trọng trước nguy hiểm
example: She was wary of making promises she might not be able to keep.

definition: Seeming reasonable or probable.
vietnamese: Có vẻ hợp lý; đáng tin; có thể xảy ra
example: The detective found the suspect's alibi plausible but decided to investigate further.

definition: To support or strengthen.
vietnamese: Củng cố; tăng cường; hỗ trợ thêm
example: Additional funding was approved to bolster the struggling public health system.

definition: No longer produced or used; out of date.
vietnamese: Lỗi thời; không còn sử dụng; lạc hậu
example: The rapid pace of technological change renders many skills obsolete within years.

definition: Truthful and straightforward; frank.
vietnamese: Thẳng thắn; trung thực; bộc trực
example: She appreciated his candid assessment of her work, even though it was difficult to hear.

definition: Having or showing the ability to speak fluently and coherently.
vietnamese: Diễn đạt rõ ràng; nói lưu loát; mạch lạc
example: The articulate spokesperson was able to explain the complex policy in simple terms.

definition: Serving as a desirable model; representing the best of its kind.
vietnamese: Gương mẫu; mẫu mực; tiêu biểu đáng noi theo
example: Her exemplary conduct throughout the investigation set a high standard for others.

definition: Anxious or fearful that something bad will happen.
vietnamese: Lo lắng; e ngại; lo sợ điều xấu sắp xảy ra
example: She was apprehensive about the surgery despite the doctor's reassurances.

definition: Giving a favorable sign of future success.
vietnamese: Tốt lành; hứa hẹn; mang điềm tốt cho tương lai
example: Winning their first match was an auspicious start to the tournament for the young team.

definition: Showing or feeling no interest, enthusiasm, or concern.
vietnamese: Thờ ơ; không quan tâm; thiếu nhiệt tình
example: An apathetic electorate leads to low voter turnout and weakened democracy.

definition: Not important or significant.
vietnamese: Không quan trọng; không đáng kể; tầm thường
example: The minor formatting errors were inconsequential to the overall quality of the report.

definition: Holding fast; persistent; not easily giving up.
vietnamese: Kiên cường; bền bỉ; không dễ bỏ cuộc
example: The tenacious investigative journalist spent five years exposing the corruption case.

definition: To go in different directions from a common point.
vietnamese: Phân kỳ; đi theo hướng khác nhau; tách ra
example: Their opinions began to diverge sharply after the policy debate.

definition: To make something clear; to explain.
vietnamese: Làm sáng tỏ; giải thích rõ ràng; thuyết minh
example: The professor elucidated the complex theory using a series of practical examples.

definition: Not resulting from deliberate planning; accidental.
vietnamese: Vô tình; không cố ý; xảy ra do sơ ý
example: The inadvertent release of sensitive data caused a major public relations crisis.

definition: To express grief, regret, or sorrow.
vietnamese: Than thở; ai oán; bày tỏ nỗi đau buồn hoặc hối tiếc
example: She lamented the loss of the ancient library, calling it an irreplaceable cultural tragedy.

definition: Just beginning to exist or develop.
vietnamese: Mới nảy sinh; đang hình thành; ở giai đoạn đầu phát triển
example: The nascent technology shows great promise but is not yet ready for commercial use.

definition: To destroy utterly; to wipe out completely.
vietnamese: Xóa sạch; phá hủy hoàn toàn; tiêu diệt triệt để
example: The hurricane obliterated entire neighborhoods, leaving nothing but rubble.

definition: A statement or situation that contradicts itself but may be true.
vietnamese: Nghịch lý; điều tự mâu thuẫn nhưng có thể đúng
example: The paradox of freedom is that it requires responsibility to function properly.

definition: Complaining in a petulant or whining manner.
vietnamese: Hay phàn nàn; lằng nhằng; thường xuyên ca thán
example: The querulous customer demanded to speak with a manager over a minor inconvenience.

definition: To remedy or set right; compensation for a wrong.
vietnamese: Bồi thường; khắc phục; sửa chữa sai lầm
example: Citizens sought legal redress after the government's policies damaged their livelihoods.

definition: Briefly and clearly expressed.
vietnamese: Súc tích; ngắn gọn mà đầy đủ ý nghĩa
example: His succinct summary captured all the key points in just three sentences.

definition: Thin, weak, or insubstantial.
vietnamese: Mong manh; yếu ớt; không chắc chắn
example: The tenuous evidence was insufficient to justify an arrest.

definition: To damage or weaken something gradually.
vietnamese: Phá hoại ngầm; làm suy yếu dần dần
example: Constant criticism from leadership can undermine employees' confidence and productivity.

definition: Using more words than necessary; wordy.
vietnamese: Dài dòng; nhiều lời; dùng nhiều từ hơn cần thiết
example: The verbose contract was condensed from 80 pages to a clearer 20-page document.

definition: Having or showing great energy or enthusiasm.
vietnamese: Nhiệt thành; hăng hái; đầy nhiệt huyết
example: The zealous volunteer worked 12-hour days to ensure the campaign's success.

definition: Something different from what is normal or expected.
vietnamese: Sự bất thường; điều không phù hợp với quy luật chung
example: The unusually high test scores were flagged as an anomaly requiring further investigation.

definition: Wary and unwilling to take risks.
vietnamese: Thận trọng; cẩn thận; không muốn mạo hiểm
example: She was circumspect in her investment decisions, always researching thoroughly before committing.

definition: To publicly declare to be wrong or evil.
vietnamese: Tố cáo; lên án công khai; tuyên bố điều gì đó là sai hoặc có hại
example: World leaders unanimously denounced the attack as an act of terror.

definition: Difficult to find, catch, or achieve.
vietnamese: Khó nắm bắt; khó đạt được; né tránh khéo léo
example: Despite decades of research, a cure for the common cold remains elusive.

definition: Sparing or economical with money or food.
vietnamese: Tiết kiệm; dè xẻn; sử dụng tiền bạc hoặc tài nguyên một cách căn cơ
example: Living a frugal lifestyle allowed her to retire comfortably at age fifty.

definition: Uncalled for; lacking good reason; unwarranted.
vietnamese: Không cần thiết; không có lý do chính đáng; thừa thãi
example: Critics objected to the film's gratuitous violence, which added nothing to the story.

definition: To pay attention to; to take notice of as a warning.
vietnamese: Chú ý; lắng nghe cảnh báo; để tâm đến lời khuyên
example: If only he had heeded the warning signs, the accident could have been avoided.

definition: Anger or annoyance provoked by what is perceived as unfair.
vietnamese: Sự phẫn nộ; tức giận vì điều bất công
example: Public indignation over the verdict led to large protests across the country.

definition: Having, showing, or done with good judgment.
vietnamese: Sáng suốt; có phán đoán tốt; thận trọng và khôn ngoan
example: A judicious allocation of resources helped the nonprofit survive the economic downturn.

definition: To act in an excessively submissive way.
vietnamese: Khúm núm; khuất phục thái quá; nịnh hót
example: He refused to kowtow to pressure from wealthy donors and maintained his independent position.

definition: Using very few words.
vietnamese: Nói ít; ngắn gọn cô đọng; kiệm lời
example: His laconic reply of 'fine' told her nothing about what had really happened.

definition: To speak about in a spitefully critical manner.
vietnamese: Nói xấu; phỉ báng; chỉ trích một cách ác ý
example: It is unfair to malign someone based on rumors without verifying the facts.

definition: Wicked or criminal.
vietnamese: Gian ác; tội lỗi; cực kỳ xấu xa về mặt đạo đức
example: The investigation uncovered the organization's nefarious financial dealings.

definition: Not straightforward; indirect.
vietnamese: Gián tiếp; không thẳng thắn; ẩn ý
example: His oblique references to the scandal made it clear he knew more than he was saying.

definition: Lasting or existing for a long or apparently infinite time.
vietnamese: Lâu dài; tồn tại mãi mãi; năm nào cũng xuất hiện
example: Traffic congestion remains a perennial problem in the world's largest cities.

definition: A state of perplexity or uncertainty.
vietnamese: Tình huống khó xử; lưỡng lự; không biết phải làm gì
example: She found herself in a quandary when both job offers arrived on the same day.

definition: Filled or well-supplied with something.
vietnamese: Đầy ắp; dư dả; sung mãn
example: The library was replete with rare manuscripts and first-edition volumes.

definition: Having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment.
vietnamese: Sáng suốt; khôn ngoan; có khả năng phán đoán sắc bén
example: The sagacious investor saw the market downturn coming months before it happened.

definition: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.
vietnamese: Ít nói; trầm lặng; không thích bộc lộ
example: The taciturn professor spoke only when he had something truly important to say.

definition: The fact of appearing everywhere.
vietnamese: Sự hiện diện khắp nơi; tình trạng phổ biến toàn diện
example: The ubiquity of smartphones has transformed how people communicate and access information.

definition: Having or showing a lack of thought or intelligence.
vietnamese: Trống rỗng về trí tuệ; nhạt nhẽo; thiếu chiều sâu suy nghĩ
example: The vacuous celebrity interview contained nothing of substance or interest.

definition: Having or showing a feeling of vague longing or regret.
vietnamese: Bâng khuâng; man mác; mang nỗi nhớ hoặc tiếc nuối mơ hồ
example: She gazed wistfully at old photographs, remembering simpler times.

definition: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries.
vietnamese: Bài ngoại; thù ghét hoặc kỳ thị người nước ngoài
example: Rising xenophobia in the region has made it harder for refugees to find safe haven.

definition: To give way or produce a result.
vietnamese: Nhường bộ; sản sinh; tạo ra kết quả
example: Years of patient research finally yielded a breakthrough in cancer treatment.

definition: Great energy or enthusiasm for a cause.
vietnamese: Sự nhiệt tình; hăng hái; niềm đam mê cho một mục tiêu
example: The volunteers worked with remarkable zeal to rebuild homes after the disaster.
